'茨城県稲敷郡美浦村郷中2988番地', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '茨城県稲敷郡美浦村木原', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Tatenui Shrine (Miho, Ibaraki)}} [[JA:楯縫神社 (美浦村郷中)]] {{wikidata link|Q17211507}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = 楯縫神社 |画像 =[[File:Tatenui Shrine (Gochu, Miura Village, Ibaraki Prefecture) 01.jpg|250px|楯縫神社（美浦村郷中）]]
拝殿 |所在地 = {{jalink|茨城県|Q83273}}{{jalink|稲敷郡|Q1154673}}{{jalink|美浦村|Q1203179}}郷中2988番地 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = 普都主命 |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}（小）
旧{{jalink|県社}} |創建 = {{jalink|推古天皇|Q298057}}16年（{{jalink|608年|Q31037}}） |本殿 = |別名 = {{jalink|信太郡|Q11386034}}{{jalink|一宮|Q1656379}} |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|5月15日|Q2562}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Ibaraki|label=楯縫神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''楯縫神社'''（たてぬいじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|茨城県|Q83273}}{{jalink|稲敷郡|Q1154673}}{{jalink|美浦村|Q1203179}}郷中2988番地[http://www.oyashiro.or.jp/ 茨城県神社庁]の神社台帳では、美浦村木原2988番地。（旧{{jalink|信太郡|Q11386034}}{{jalink|木原村|Q11518209}}）にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。古名は{{jalink|信太郡|Q11386034}}{{jalink|一宮|Q1656379}}。{{jalink|式内社}}（{{jalink|常陸国|Q1055353}}信太郡、小社）。旧{{jalink|県社}}。美浦村信太にも同名の楯縫神社がある。 === 概要 === [[File:Tatenui Shrine (Gochu, Miura Village, Ibaraki Prefecture) 03.jpg|thumb|一の鳥居]] [[File:Tatenui-jinja shrine Ni no Torii Gate (Miho village, Ibaraki prefecture).jpg|thumb|二の鳥居]] [[File:Tatenui Shrine (Gochu, Miura Village, Ibaraki Prefecture) 02.jpg|thumb|手水舎]] 参道は{{jalink|国道125号|Q6156756}}現道に面しているが、境内は奥まった場所にあるため発見しにくい。巨樹林立する樹叢に、長大な参道が通り、奥部に社殿を構えている。社務所や目立った境内社はなく、樹叢と社殿による素朴な構図を楽しむことができる。 境内は茨城県指定{{jalink|自然環境保全地域|Q11612866}}（29、一の宮）。{{jalink|常陸国風土記|Q11481687}}の信太郡の条の普都神話常陸国風土記の原文は「高来里、古老曰、天地權輿、草木言語之時、自天降来神名、称'''普都大神'''、巡行葦原中津国、和平山河荒梗之類、大神化道已畢、心存帰天、即時随身'''器杖（俗曰、伊川乃川惠）甲戈楯剣及所執玉珪、悉皆脱履'''、留置茲地、即乗白雲、還昇蒼天」。 に描かれている、{{jalink|経津主神|Q1107286|普都大神}}が楯を脱いだ地、すなわち「楯脱（楯縫）」の聖地とされている。木原の地名由来伝承には二説あるが、いずれも一帯が往古から豊かな森であったことを伝えている。 普都神話の聖地の結び付きから{{jalink|阿彌神社 (阿見町竹来)|Q17191825|竹来阿彌神社}}と関係が深く、往古は信太郡東西の総社として一宮二宮を称し、ともに{{jalink|永和 (日本)|Q1145780|永和}}元年（1375年）の円密院寺社供僧等言上状に「庄内第一之惣廟」と書かれた大日本地名辞典。。楯縫神社は信太郡一宮として、旧郡域一帯では筆頭の格式にあった。普都主命の{{jalink|神輿|Q979940}}を霞ヶ浦を介して{{jalink|阿彌神社|Q16276818}}に渡御する古式祭（鹿島神事）も行っていた。 === 祭神 === ; 主祭神 : 普都主命明治神社誌料。（普津主神、普都主大神） ; 配祀神 : {{jalink|大国主|Q276944|大己貴命}}二座、{{jalink|スサノオ|Q272993|須佐之男命}}、{{jalink|ウカノミタマ|Q3080728|宇迦魂命}}二座、皇産靈命、{{jalink|イチキシマヒメ|Q11059454|市杵島姫命}}、{{jalink|熊野大社|Q11568887|熊野加夫呂岐命}} * 境内案内板では{{jalink|記紀|Q842308}}{{jalink|神話|Q12827256}}における{{jalink|フツヌシ||經津主神}}の事績を紹介し、二座を同一神格としている（普都大神については{{jalink|阿彌神社 (阿見町竹来)|Q17191825|竹来阿彌神社}}も参照）。 * 主祭神を{{jalink|彦狭知命|Q135327097}}とする別伝もある。 * 神社整理による合併は以下茨城県神社写真帳。。 ** {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}41年、大字須賀津の厳島神社（市杵島姫命）を合併。 ** 明治44年、八幡神社（{{jalink|誉田別命}}）、愛宕神社（火産霊命二社）を境内社御龍社に合併。 ** 明治45年、稲荷神社（宇賀御魂命）、折居神社（{{jalink|淤加美神|Q644201|高龍命}}）、厳島神社（市杵島姫命）を境内社御龍社に合併。 === 由緒 === 社伝と別伝がある。また、創建については二説以外に紀元18年説がある茨城県常陸国風土記1300年記念。。 === 社伝 === * 創建は{{jalink|推古天皇|Q298057}}16年（608年）境内案内板。。 * 普都神話に創祀の縁起を求める。曰く、普都大神は、{{jalink|葦原中国平定}}の後、木原で甲楯を脱ぎ、高来里で登天した。甲楯を脱いだことから「楯脱」の地名が生まれ、後に「楯縫」になった。社地には「楯脱山」の地名が残る。 * 木原の村名は、{{jalink|永正|Q1193577}}3年（1506年）、境内にあった周囲5丈8尺余、高さ120尺の神代{{jalink|スギ|Q147388|杉}}（{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}元年5月（1868年）枯死）の「木」と、{{jalink|木原城 (常陸国)|Q11518195|木原城}}主近藤式部大輔藤原利勝（近藤利勝）の「原」を{{jalink|合成地名|Q11412460|合成}}して生まれた。それまでは神越村と称していたという境内案内板、明治神社誌料等。美浦村教育委員会の文化財解説では、境内の北にある周囲6メートル余の巨杉が由来としている。。 === 別伝 === * 新編常陸国誌は「祭神{{jalink|彦狭知命|Q135327097}}と云伝ふ、郡中東33村の鎮守にして、即本郡の一宮也（社記、二十八社考、二十八社略縁起）、この神は神代の時に、{{jalink|紀伊国|Q864053}}の{{jalink|忌部}}祖、{{jalink|手置帆負神}}と同く、{{jalink|天照大神|Q455602}}の御為に、瑞の御殿を造り、又諸の祭器を作り仕奉りしが、この神は専ら盾を縫ひ作られし故に、{{jalink|盾縫|Q11582552|楯縫}}神とも申せしなり（日本紀、古語拾遺）人代にも祭器用の盾を作る楯部がいた。」（原文カナ）とし、「楯縫神」たる彦狭知命を祀る社としている。 * 木原の村名は「古老相伝云、上古此処茫々たる荒原にして、杉檜処々に生ず、因りて木原と号く、或時一人の翁忽然と出現し、里人に告げて曰、汝等家屋甚だ拙し、此の宮を見て作り立つべしと、一つ尺度を授けて、翁は杉の小陰に入り玉ひぬ、里人再拝教に従て、神前に群参し、此里に家居を始むと云り（二十八社略縁起）」（原文カナ）とし、木原集落の形成が楯縫神社とともにあったことも伝えている。 * 稲敷郡郷土史には「維新前此は説により誰云ふともなく楯縫神社は大工の祖神なりと称し参拝せしものありしと」と、明治以前まで別伝がそれなりに伝播していたことを示唆する記述がある。 * 一般には、楯縫神社を称する社の祭神は彦狹知命である。楯縫神社または楯部の式内社は、当社以外に少なくとも四社あり、いずれも「楯縫神（彦狹知命）」の社だった。 ** 楯縫神社（{{jalink|但馬国|Q1053727}}{{jalink|養父郡 (兵庫県)|Q11666859|養父郡}}、小社）。兵庫県養父市。主祭神・彦狹知命 ** 楯縫神社（但馬国{{jalink|気多郡 (兵庫県)|Q21655271|気多郡}}、小社）。兵庫県豊岡市。主祭神・彦狹知命 ** 楯縫神社（{{jalink|丹波国|Q954511}}{{jalink|氷上郡|Q5760344}}、小社）。兵庫県丹波市。主祭神・彦狹知命 ** 川内多々奴比神社（丹波国{{jalink|多紀郡|Q11431006}}、小社）。兵庫県丹波市。主祭神・天照皇大御神
「多々奴比」は「楯縫」が転訛したもので、元は楯部の社として彦狹知命を祀っていたという。 * {{jalink|特選神名牒|Q11571092|特選神名帳}}は「今按、楯縫の社号によるときは、彦狭知命を祭れるが如くなれども、{{jalink|出雲国風土記|Q11395858}}{{jalink|意宇郡|Q11073594}}楯縫郷の条に、布都怒志命之天石楯縫直給レ之、故云二楯縫一武田祐吉編による読み下しは「布都怒志（ふつぬし）の命、天の石楯（あめのいはたて）を縫い直し給ひき。故、楯縫といふ」。とあるに、社説を合せて、經津主命なることしるへし」と、「楯縫」の称が必ずしも「楯縫神（彦狭知命）」に由来するわけではないと記している。 === 沿革 === * {{jalink|神領}}53町余を持っていたが、{{jalink|興国|Q1143592}}元年（1340年）、{{jalink|北朝 (日本)|Q1201819|北朝}}の{{jalink|高師冬|Q6455071}}の部下屋代信経による侵攻を受け無禄になった。 * {{jalink|天文 (元号)|Q1192853|天文}}年間（1532-1555年）、木造狛犬（美浦村指定文化財）奉納と伝えられている。 * {{jalink|文明 (日本)|Q1193544|文明}}8年（1476年）、{{jalink|天正|Q1145058}}17年（1589年）、{{jalink|嘉永|Q1146125}}7年（1854年）に再建記録あり。現本殿は天正17年造立、近藤利勝の寄進と伝えられている。 * 近世、信太郡東半33郷50余村の総社として信太郡一宮を称した。 * {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}6年（1873年）、県社列格。 * {{jalink|昭和|Q327567}}40年（1965年）、拝殿焼失により古文書等を喪失常陽リビング。。 === 周辺の神社 === ; 楯縫神社 : 茨城県稲敷郡美浦村信太1830番地（旧信太郡信太村） : 主祭神・経津主命 : 配祀神・{{jalink|タカミムスビ||高皇産霊神}}、{{jalink|菅原道真|Q382005|菅原道真公}}、{{jalink|誉田別命}}、武甕槌命、{{jalink|コノハナサクヤヒメ||木花開耶比売命}}、宇迦之御魂命 :* 配祀神6柱は神社整理による合祀神（皇産霊神社、天満神社、香取神社、八幡神社、二宮鹿島神社、浅間神社、宇賀神社）。 :* 創建不詳。社伝では{{jalink|景行天皇|Q329723}}御代（不詳）創建、{{jalink|霊亀|Q1373115}}元年（715年）再建稲敷郡郷土史。。旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}。神社は式内楯縫神社の比定社、及び信太郡惣社（扁額）としている。 :* 信太城址の東北にあり、一帯は信太{{jalink|郡衙|Q11643230}}の候補地でもある。 === 交通 === JR土浦駅からJRバス関東に乗車、バス停「郷中」下車、徒歩10分 === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} === 参考文献 === * [https://www.ndl.go.jp/ 国立国会図書館]：[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/ 国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション]より閲覧可能な文献。 ** 中山信名, 栗田寛編「新編常陸国誌 巻下」。積善館。明治32-34年（1899-1901年） ** 吉田東伍「大日本地名辞典 下巻 二版」。冨山房。明治40年10月17日（1907年）。 ** 明治神社誌料編纂所編「明治神社誌料 府県郷社（上）」。明治神社誌料編纂所。明治45年（1912年） ** 塙泉嶺編「稲敷郡郷土史 附名誉鑑」。宗教新聞社。大正15年（1926年）。 ** 武田祐吉編「風土記」岩波書店、岩波文庫。昭和12年（1937年）。 ** いはらき新聞「茨城県神社写真帳」。いはらき新聞社。昭和16年（1942年）。 * [http://www.bunkajoho.pref.ibaraki.jp/index_fudoki.html 茨城県常陸国風土記1300年記念]：[http://www.bunkajoho.pref.ibaraki.jp/fudoki/visit/32/index.html 楯縫神社]。2017年3月20日閲覧。 * [http://www.joyoliving.co.jp/index.html 常陽リビング]：[http://www.joyoliving.co.jp/special/heritage/00040/ 茨城歴史散歩「楯縫神社の狛犬 - 美浦村」]。2009年5月20日。2013年11月15日閲覧。 {{神道 横}} {{茨城県の神社一覧目次}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たてぬいしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tatenui Shrine (Miho, Ibaraki)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tatenui Shrine (Miho, Ibaraki)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Tatenui Shrine (Miho, Ibaraki)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:茨城県の神社]] [[Category:茨城県の旧県社]] [[Category:常陸国の式内社]] [[Category:美浦村の建築物]] [[Category:美浦村の歴史]] [[Category:一宮 (地域)]] [[Category:信太郡]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:kkkkkkkkkkkkkk]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qq]]

{{Expand Japanese|楯縫神社 (美浦村郷中)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Tatenui Shrine (Miho, Ibaraki) | native_name = 楯縫神社 | image = Tatenui Shrine (Gochu, Miura Village, Ibaraki Prefecture) 01.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Tatenui Shrine (Miho, Ibaraki) | coordinates = {{coord|36.013282|140.303055}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] }} {{nihongo|'''Tatenui Shrine (Miho, Ibaraki)'''|楯縫神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. '茨城県稲敷郡美浦村郷中2988番地', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '茨城県稲敷郡美浦村木原', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Tatenui Shrine (Miho, Ibaraki)}} [[JA:楯縫神社 (美浦村郷中)]] {{wikidata link|Q17211507}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Tatenui Shrine |Image =[[File:Tatenui Shrine (Gochu, Miura Village, Ibaraki Prefecture) 01.jpg|250px|Tatenui Shrine (Miura Village Gochu)]]
Oratory |Location = {{jalink|Ibaraki Prefecture|Q83273}}{{jalink|Inashiki District|Q1154673}}{{jalink|Miho Village|Q1203179}}Gochu 2988 |Position = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = Futoshi no Mikoto |Company rank = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small)
Old {{jalink|Prefectural company}} |Foundation = {{jalink|Emperor Suiko|Q298057}}16th year ({{jalink|608th year|Q31037}}) |Main shrine = |Alias = {{jalink|Shota County|Q11386034}}{{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q1656379}} |Fudasho, etc. = |Regular festival = {{jalink|May 15th|Q2562}} |Shinto ritual = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Ibaraki|label=Tatenui Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tatenui Shrine'' (Tatenui Shrine) is located at 2988 Gochu [http://www.oyashiro.or.jp/ According to the shrine register of the Ibaraki Prefectural Shrine Office, it is 2988 Kihara, Miura Village. (formerly {{jalink|Shota District|Q11386034}}{{jalink|Kihara Village|Q11518209}}) {{jalink|Shrine|Q845945}}. The old name was {{jalink|Shota-gun|Q11386034}}{{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q1656379}}. {{jalink|Shikiuchi-sha}} ({{jalink|Hitachi-kuni|Q1055353}} Shinta District, small shrine). Former {{jalink|kensha}}. There is also Tatenui Shrine with the same name in Shinta Miura Village. === Overview === [[File:Tatenui Shrine (Gochu, Miura Village, Ibaraki Prefecture) 03.jpg|thumb|First Torii]] [[File:Tatenui-jinja shrine Ni no Torii Gate (Miho village, Ibaraki prefecture).jpg|thumb|Ni no Torii Gate]] [[File:Tatenui Shrine (Gochu, Miura Village, Ibaraki Prefecture) 02.jpg|thumb|Chozusha]] The approach road faces the {{jalink|National Route 125|Q6156756}} existing road, but the shrine grounds are located in a secluded area and are difficult to find. A long approach path passes through a forest of giant trees, and the shrine building is located at the back. There are no shrine offices or prominent shrines within the precincts, so you can enjoy the simple composition of the grove of trees and shrine buildings.The precincts are designated by Ibaraki Prefecture {{jalink|natural environment conservation area|Q11612866}} (29, Ichinomiya). {{jalink|Hitachikuni Fudoki|Q11481687}} Fudu myth in the Shinta district sectionThe original text of Hitachikuni Fudoki is ``Takakiri, Koroi, Tenchi Kinkoshi, Plants and trees language no time, the name of the deity who descended from heaven, the title '''Futu Great God''', Junko Ashihara Nakatsukuni, Hehei Mountain River Arakyo no Rui, Great God Transformation Dojinbi, Mind Returns to Heaven, Immediate Zuishin''' Equipment Staff (commonly said, Ikawanogawa Mei), Armored Shield and Shogyokukei, All Take Off''', Detained Kageji, Immediately Riding Hakuun, Returning Soten. It is said to be the place where {{jalink|Ketsunushikami|Q1107286|Futsu Okami}} took off his shield, which is depicted in , and is therefore the sacred place of ``Tatenui''. There are two legends about the origin of the name Kihara, but both tell that the area has been a rich forest since ancient times. It has a deep relationship with {{jalink|Ami Shrine (Takeki, Ami Town)|Q17191825|Takekiami Shrine}} due to its association with the sacred place of Futsu mythology, and in ancient times it was called Ichinomiya Ninomiya as the main shrine for east and west Shinta District, and both {{jalink|Eiwa (Japan)|Q1145780|Eiwa}} 1st year (1375) of the Enmyin-ji Shrine and the Shrine and the Sobō Dojojo, which reads "Shonai Daiichi-no-Sobyo" Great Japanese Place Name Dictionary. . Tatenui Shrine was the Shinta District Ichinomiya Shrine, and was one of the most prestigious shrines in the former district area. An ancient ceremony (Kashima Shinto ritual) was also held in which the {{jalink|mikoshi|Q979940}} of Futsu no Mikoto was carried to {{jalink|Ami Shrine|Q16276818}} via Lake Kasumigaura. === Enshrined God === ; Main enshrined deity : Futsu no MikotoMeiji Shrine materials. (Futsusu no Kami, Futsusu Okami) ; Deity enshrined : {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Otomi Takamikoto}} Niza, {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Susanoo no Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Ukanomitama|Q308072 8|Ukatama no Mikoto}} Niza, Kosan Reimyo, {{jalink|Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|Ichikishima Hime no Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Kumano Taisha|Q11568887|Kumano Kaoroki no Mikoto}} * The precinct information board introduces the deeds of {{jalink|Futunushi||Ami Shrine}} in {{jalink|kiki|Q842308}}{{jalink|mythology|Q12827256}}, and considers the two deities to be the same deity (for Futsu Okami, please refer to {{jalink|Ami Shrine (Also see Takeki, Ami Town)|Q17191825|Takekuami Shrine}}). * There is also a separate legend that says the main enshrined deity is {{jalink|Hikosa Tomomi|Q135327097}}. * The merger due to shrine reorganization is below Ibaraki Prefecture Shrine Photo Book. . ** {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}}In 1941, merged with Itsukushima Shrine (Ichikishima Hime-no-Mikoto) in Sukazu, Oaza. ** In 1907, Hachiman Shrine ({{jalink|Honda Betsu no Mikoto}}) and Atago Shrine (Housan Reimyo Nisha) were merged into the precinct shrine Goryusha. ** In Meiji 45, Inari Shrine (Ugamitama no Mikoto), Orii Shrine ({{jalink|Yokamijin|Q644201|Koryu no Mikoto}}), and Itsukushima Shrine (Ichikishimahime no Mikoto) were merged into the precinct shrine Goryusha. === History === There is a company biography and a separate biography. In addition to the two theories about its founding, there is another theory that says it was built in 18 AD. . === Company biography === * Founded in {{jalink|Emperor Suiko|Q298057}}16 (608)Precinct information board. . * Searching for the auspicious origin of creation rituals in Fudu mythology. It is said that after the {{jalink|pacification of Ashihara China}}, Futsu Okami took off his armor at Kihara and ascended to heaven at Takakiri. Because he took off his armor, the place name ``Tatezu'' was born, and later became ``Tatenui.'' The place name ``Tatezuzan'' still remains on the shrine grounds. * The name of the village of Kihara is derived from {{jalink|Eisho|Q1193577}} 3rd year (1506), which was built in the precincts of a temple with a circumference of 5 jo, 8 shaku, and a height of 120 shaku.{{jalink |Sugi|Q147388|Cedar}} ({{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} withered in May 1868) and {{jalink|Kihara Castle (Hitachi Province)|Q11518195|Kihara Castle}}It was born by {{jalink|combined place name|Q11412460|combined}} of the ``Hara'' of the lord Kondo Shikibu Daisuke Fujiwara Toshikatsu (Kondo Toshikatsu). Until then, it was called Kamikoshi Village.Precinct information board, Meiji Shrine magazine materials, etc. According to the Miho Village Board of Education's explanation of the cultural property, the origin is a giant cedar tree with a circumference of more than 6 meters located in the north of the precincts. . === Separate story === * The new edition of Hitachi Kokushi states, ``He is said to be the enshrined deity {{jalink|Tomomichi Hikosa|Q135327097}}, the guardian of 33 villages in the Middle East of the district, and Ichinomiya of Sokuhon District (shaki, 28 shrines, 28 shrines summary origin) , this god was known as Amaterasu, the ancestor of {{jalink|Ibe}} of {{jalink|Kioku|Q864053}}, as well as {{jalink|Tegiho Negami}}, during the age of the gods. For the great god |Q455602}}, he built a magnificent palace and made various ritual vessels to serve, but since this god only sewed shields, {{jalink|shield sewing|Q11582552|shield sewing} }}It is also said to be a god (Nihongi, Kogo Shui) Even in the human era, there was a tatebe who made shields for ritual utensils.'' (original kana), and the shrine is said to enshrine Hikosa Tomomichi, the ``tatenui deity.'' * The name of the village of Kihara is said to be from an ancient legend that says, ``In the old ancient times, this place was a vast wasteland, with cedar and cypress trees growing here and there, hence the name Kihara.One day, an old man suddenly appeared and told the villagers, ``Your houses are very poor, and when you see this palace, you should build one.'' The old man went under the shade of a cedar tree, entered Tamahinu, followed the worship of the villagers, gathered in front of the shrine, and started his home in this village (28 shrines abbreviated engagement)' (original kana), and it is also said that the Kihara village was formed along with Tatenui Shrine. * In the local history of Inashiki District, there is a statement that says, ``Before the Meiji Restoration, there was a theory that people visited Tatenui Shrine and worshiped it, calling it the ancestral deity of carpenters,'' which suggests that a separate legend had spread in its own way until before the Meiji period. *Generally, the deity enshrined at the shrine called Tatenui Shrine is Hikono Tomomichi. In addition to our company, there were at least four other Tatenui Shrines or Tatebe Shikinai shrines, all of which were shrines of the ``Tatenui God'' (Hikono Chimei).** Tatenui Shrine ({{jalink|Tajima Province|Q1053727}}{{jalink|Yabu District (Hyogo Prefecture)|Q11666859|Yabu District}}, small shrine). Yabu City, Hyogo Prefecture. Main enshrined deity, Hikono Tomomi ** Tatenui Shrine (Tajima Province {{jalink|Keta District (Hyogo Prefecture)|Q21655271|Keta District}}, small shrine). Toyooka City, Hyogo Prefecture. Main enshrined deity, Hikono Tomomi ** Tatenui Shrine ({{jalink|Tamba Province|Q954511}}{{jalink|Hikami District|Q5760344}}, small shrine). Tamba City, Hyogo Prefecture. Main enshrined deity, Hikono Tomomi ** Kawauchi Tatanuhi Shrine (Tamba Province {{jalink|Taki District|Q11431006}}, small shrine). Tamba City, Hyogo Prefecture. Main enshrined deity: Amaterasu Omikami
``Tadatanohi'' is a corruption of ``Tatenui,'' and it is said that it was originally a shrine of Tatebe that enshrined Hikonochi no Mikoto. * {{jalink|Specially Selected Names of Gods|Q11571092|Specially Selected Names of Gods}} states, ``If the shrine name of Tatenui is used now, it would be as if Hikosa Chi no Mikoto could be enshrined; In the article, ``Futunushi no Mikoto re-sewn the heavenly stone shield (Ame no Ihatate). Therefore, it is called ``Tatenui.'''' edited by Yukichi Takeda. Together with the editorial, it is written that the name ``Tatenui'' is not necessarily derived from ``Tatenui no Mikoto'' (Hikosatomo no Mikoto). === History === * {{jalink|Shinryo}} had over 53 towns, but in the first year of {{jalink|Kokoku|Q1143592}} (1340), it was invaded by Nobutsune, a subordinate of {{jalink|Koshifuyu|Q6455071}} of {{jalink|Northern Court (Japan)|Q1201819|Northern Court}}, and became without a living. * It is said that a wooden guardian dog (a cultural property designated by Miho Village) was dedicated to the for the {{jalink|Tenbun (era name)|Q1192853|Tenbun}} years (1532-1555). * There are records of reconstruction in {{jalink|Bunmei (Japan)|Q1193544|Bunmei}}8 (1476), {{jalink|Tensho|Q1145058}}17 (1589), and {{jalink|Kaei|Q1146125}}7 (1854). The current main hall is said to have been built in Tensho 17 and donated by Toshikatsu Kondo. * In early modern times, Shinta County Ichinomiya was called the shrine of more than 50 villages in the eastern half of Shinta County. * {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}}6 (1873), ranked as a prefectural company. * {{jalink|Showa|Q327567}}In 1965, the temple of worship was burnt down and ancient documents were lostJoyo Living. . === Nearby shrines === ; Tatenui Shrine : 1830 Shinta, Miho Village, Inashiki District, Ibaraki Prefecture (formerly Shinta Village, Shinta District) : Main enshrined deity, Kyotsu no Mikoto : Deities enshrined: {{jalink|Takamimusubi||Takōsei Reishin}}, {{jalink|Michizane Sugawara|Q382005|Michizane Sugawara}}, {{jalink|Honda Betsu no Mikoto}}, Takemikazuchi no Mikoto, {{jalink|Konohana Sakuyahime||Konohana Kaiyahime no Mikoto}}, Uka no Mitama no Mikoto :* The six deities enshrined are jointly enshrined deities (Kousanrei Shrine, Tenma Shrine, Katori Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Ninomiya Kashima Shrine, Sengen Shrine, and Uga Shrine). :* Founding unknown. According to the shrine's biography, it was founded in the {{jalink|Emperor Keiko|Q329723}} reign (unknown) and rebuilt in the first year (715) of {{jalink|Reiki|Q1373115}}Local history of Inashiki district. . Old {{jalink|Murasha|Q134917284}}. The shrines are Hijo-sha, Shikinai Tatenui-jinja Shrine, and Shinta-gun Sosha (framed). :* Located to the northeast of Shinta Castle Ruins, the area is also a candidate site for Shinta {{jalink|gunga|Q11643230}}. === Transportation === Take the JR Bus Kanto from JR Tsuchiura Station, get off at the "Gonaka" bus stop, and walk 10 minutes. === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist|2}} === References === * [https://www.ndl.go.jp/ National Diet Library]: Documents available for viewing from [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/ National Diet Library Digital Collection]. ** Nobuna Nakayama, Hiroshi Kurita, eds. “New edition of Hitachi Kokushi, Vol. 2.” Sekizenkan. Meiji 32-34 (1899-1901) ** Togo Yoshida, “Great Japanese Place Name Dictionary, Volume 2, 2nd Edition.” Toyamabo. October 17, 1907. ** "Meiji Shrine Archives Prefectural Gosha (Part 1)" edited by Meiji Shrine Archives Editorial Office. Meiji Shrine Materials Editorial Office. 1912 (Meiji 45) ** "Inashiki County Local History Honorary Book" edited by Izumi Hanawa. Religious newspaper company. Taisho 15 (1926). ** “Fudoki” edited by Yukichi Takeda, Iwanami Shoten, Iwanami Bunko. 1937. ** Iharaki Shimbun "Ibaraki Prefecture Shrine Photo Book". Iharaki Shimbunsha. 1942. * [http://www.bunkajoho.pref.ibaraki.jp/index_fudoki.html Ibaraki Prefecture Hitachi Province 1300th Anniversary]: [http://www.bunkajoho.pref.ibaraki.jp/fudoki/visit/32/index.html Tatenui Shrine]. Accessed March 20, 2017. * [http://www.joyoliving.co.jp/index.html Joyo Living]: [http://www.joyoliving.co.jp/special/heritage/00040/ Ibaraki History Walk "Guardian Dogs of Tatenui Shrine - Miura Village"]. May 20, 2009. Accessed November 15, 2013.{{Shinto horizontal}} {{List of shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tate-Nui Shin-Shiya}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tatenui Shrine (Miho, Ibaraki)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tatenui Shrine (Miho, Ibaraki)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Tatenui Shrine (Miho, Ibaraki)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:茨城県の神社]] [[Category:茨城県の旧県社]] [[Category:常陸国の式内社]] [[Category:美浦村の建築物]] [[Category:美浦村の歴史]] [[Category:一宮 (地域)]] [[Category:信太郡]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:kkkkkkkkkkkkkk]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qq]]

'茨城県稲敷郡美浦村郷中2988番地', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '茨城県稲敷郡美浦村木原', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Tatenui Shrine (Miho, Ibaraki)}} [[JA:楯縫神社 (美浦村郷中)]] {{wikidata link|Q17211507}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Tatenui Shrine |Image =[[File:Tatenui Shrine (Gochu, Miura Village, Ibaraki Prefecture) 01.jpg|250px|Tatenui Shrine (Miura Village Gochu)]]
Oratory |Location = {{jalink|Ibaraki Prefecture|Q83273}}{{jalink|Inashiki District|Q1154673}}{{jalink|Miho Village|Q1203179}}Gochu 2988 |Position = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = Futoshi no Mikoto |Company rank = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small)
Old {{jalink|Prefectural company}} |Foundation = {{jalink|Emperor Suiko|Q298057}}16th year ({{jalink|608th year|Q31037}}) |Main shrine = |Alias = {{jalink|Shota County|Q11386034}}{{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q1656379}} |Fudasho, etc. = |Regular festival = {{jalink|May 15th|Q2562}} |Shinto ritual = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Ibaraki|label=Tatenui Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tatenui Shrine'' (Tatenui Shrine) is located at 2988 Gochu [http://www.oyashiro.or.jp/ According to the shrine register of the Ibaraki Prefectural Shrine Office, it is 2988 Kihara, Miura Village. (formerly {{jalink|Shota District|Q11386034}}{{jalink|Kihara Village|Q11518209}}) {{jalink|Shrine|Q845945}}. The old name was {{jalink|Shota-gun|Q11386034}}{{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q1656379}}. {{jalink|Shikiuchi-sha}} ({{jalink|Hitachi-kuni|Q1055353}} Shinta District, small shrine). Former {{jalink|kensha}}. There is also Tatenui Shrine with the same name in Shinta Miura Village. === Overview === [[File:Tatenui Shrine (Gochu, Miura Village, Ibaraki Prefecture) 03.jpg|thumb|First Torii]] [[File:Tatenui-jinja shrine Ni no Torii Gate (Miho village, Ibaraki prefecture).jpg|thumb|Ni no Torii Gate]] [[File:Tatenui Shrine (Gochu, Miura Village, Ibaraki Prefecture) 02.jpg|thumb|Chozusha]] The approach road faces the {{jalink|National Route 125|Q6156756}} existing road, but the shrine grounds are located in a secluded area and are difficult to find. A long approach path passes through a forest of giant trees, and the shrine building is located at the back. There are no shrine offices or prominent shrines within the precincts, so you can enjoy the simple composition of the grove of trees and shrine buildings.The precincts are designated by Ibaraki Prefecture {{jalink|natural environment conservation area|Q11612866}} (29, Ichinomiya). {{jalink|Hitachikuni Fudoki|Q11481687}} Fudu myth in the Shinta district sectionThe original text of Hitachikuni Fudoki is ``Takakiri, Koroi, Tenchi Kinkoshi, Plants and trees language no time, the name of the deity who descended from heaven, the title '''Futu Great God''', Junko Ashihara Nakatsukuni, Hehei Mountain River Arakyo no Rui, Great God Transformation Dojinbi, Mind Returns to Heaven, Immediate Zuishin''' Equipment Staff (commonly said, Ikawanogawa Mei), Armored Shield and Shogyokukei, All Take Off''', Detained Kageji, Immediately Riding Hakuun, Returning Soten. It is said to be the place where {{jalink|Ketsunushikami|Q1107286|Futsu Okami}} took off his shield, which is depicted in , and is therefore the sacred place of ``Tatenui''. There are two legends about the origin of the name Kihara, but both tell that the area has been a rich forest since ancient times. It has a deep relationship with {{jalink|Ami Shrine (Takeki, Ami Town)|Q17191825|Takekiami Shrine}} due to its association with the sacred place of Futsu mythology, and in ancient times it was called Ichinomiya Ninomiya as the main shrine for east and west Shinta District, and both {{jalink|Eiwa (Japan)|Q1145780|Eiwa}} 1st year (1375) of the Enmyin-ji Shrine and the Shrine and the Sobō Dojojo, which reads "Shonai Daiichi-no-Sobyo" Great Japanese Place Name Dictionary. . Tatenui Shrine was the Shinta District Ichinomiya Shrine, and was one of the most prestigious shrines in the former district area. An ancient ceremony (Kashima Shinto ritual) was also held in which the {{jalink|mikoshi|Q979940}} of Futsu no Mikoto was carried to {{jalink|Ami Shrine|Q16276818}} via Lake Kasumigaura. === Enshrined God === ; Main enshrined deity : Futsu no MikotoMeiji Shrine materials. (Futsusu no Kami, Futsusu Okami) ; Deity enshrined : {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Otomi Takamikoto}} Niza, {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Susanoo no Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Ukanomitama|Q308072 8|Ukatama no Mikoto}} Niza, Kosan Reimyo, {{jalink|Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|Ichikishima Hime no Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Kumano Taisha|Q11568887|Kumano Kaoroki no Mikoto}} * The precinct information board introduces the deeds of {{jalink|Futunushi||Ami Shrine}} in {{jalink|kiki|Q842308}}{{jalink|mythology|Q12827256}}, and considers the two deities to be the same deity (for Futsu Okami, please refer to {{jalink|Ami Shrine (Also see Takeki, Ami Town)|Q17191825|Takekuami Shrine}}). * There is also a separate legend that says the main enshrined deity is {{jalink|Hikosa Tomomi|Q135327097}}. * The merger due to shrine reorganization is below Ibaraki Prefecture Shrine Photo Book. . ** {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}}In 1941, merged with Itsukushima Shrine (Ichikishima Hime-no-Mikoto) in Sukazu, Oaza. ** In 1907, Hachiman Shrine ({{jalink|Honda Betsu no Mikoto}}) and Atago Shrine (Housan Reimyo Nisha) were merged into the precinct shrine Goryusha. ** In Meiji 45, Inari Shrine (Ugamitama no Mikoto), Orii Shrine ({{jalink|Yokamijin|Q644201|Koryu no Mikoto}}), and Itsukushima Shrine (Ichikishimahime no Mikoto) were merged into the precinct shrine Goryusha. === History === There is a company biography and a separate biography. In addition to the two theories about its founding, there is another theory that says it was built in 18 AD. . === Company biography === * Founded in {{jalink|Emperor Suiko|Q298057}}16 (608)Precinct information board. . * Searching for the auspicious origin of creation rituals in Fudu mythology. It is said that after the {{jalink|pacification of Ashihara China}}, Futsu Okami took off his armor at Kihara and ascended to heaven at Takakiri. Because he took off his armor, the place name ``Tatezu'' was born, and later became ``Tatenui.'' The place name ``Tatezuzan'' still remains on the shrine grounds. * The name of the village of Kihara is derived from {{jalink|Eisho|Q1193577}} 3rd year (1506), which was built in the precincts of a temple with a circumference of 5 jo, 8 shaku, and a height of 120 shaku.{{jalink |Sugi|Q147388|Cedar}} ({{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} withered in May 1868) and {{jalink|Kihara Castle (Hitachi Province)|Q11518195|Kihara Castle}}It was born by {{jalink|combined place name|Q11412460|combined}} of the ``Hara'' of the lord Kondo Shikibu Daisuke Fujiwara Toshikatsu (Kondo Toshikatsu). Until then, it was called Kamikoshi Village.Precinct information board, Meiji Shrine magazine materials, etc. According to the Miho Village Board of Education's explanation of the cultural property, the origin is a giant cedar tree with a circumference of more than 6 meters located in the north of the precincts. . === Separate story === * The new edition of Hitachi Kokushi states, ``He is said to be the enshrined deity {{jalink|Tomomichi Hikosa|Q135327097}}, the guardian of 33 villages in the Middle East of the district, and Ichinomiya of Sokuhon District (shaki, 28 shrines, 28 shrines summary origin) , this god was known as Amaterasu, the ancestor of {{jalink|Ibe}} of {{jalink|Kioku|Q864053}}, as well as {{jalink|Tegiho Negami}}, during the age of the gods. For the great god |Q455602}}, he built a magnificent palace and made various ritual vessels to serve, but since this god only sewed shields, {{jalink|shield sewing|Q11582552|shield sewing} }}It is also said to be a god (Nihongi, Kogo Shui) Even in the human era, there was a tatebe who made shields for ritual utensils.'' (original kana), and the shrine is said to enshrine Hikosa Tomomichi, the ``tatenui deity.'' * The name of the village of Kihara is said to be from an ancient legend that says, ``In the old ancient times, this place was a vast wasteland, with cedar and cypress trees growing here and there, hence the name Kihara.One day, an old man suddenly appeared and told the villagers, ``Your houses are very poor, and when you see this palace, you should build one.'' The old man went under the shade of a cedar tree, entered Tamahinu, followed the worship of the villagers, gathered in front of the shrine, and started his home in this village (28 shrines abbreviated engagement)' (original kana), and it is also said that the Kihara village was formed along with Tatenui Shrine. * In the local history of Inashiki District, there is a statement that says, ``Before the Meiji Restoration, there was a theory that people visited Tatenui Shrine and worshiped it, calling it the ancestral deity of carpenters,'' which suggests that a separate legend had spread in its own way until before the Meiji period. *Generally, the deity enshrined at the shrine called Tatenui Shrine is Hikono Tomomichi. In addition to our company, there were at least four other Tatenui Shrines or Tatebe Shikinai shrines, all of which were shrines of the ``Tatenui God'' (Hikono Chimei).** Tatenui Shrine ({{jalink|Tajima Province|Q1053727}}{{jalink|Yabu District (Hyogo Prefecture)|Q11666859|Yabu District}}, small shrine). Yabu City, Hyogo Prefecture. Main enshrined deity, Hikono Tomomi ** Tatenui Shrine (Tajima Province {{jalink|Keta District (Hyogo Prefecture)|Q21655271|Keta District}}, small shrine). Toyooka City, Hyogo Prefecture. Main enshrined deity, Hikono Tomomi ** Tatenui Shrine ({{jalink|Tamba Province|Q954511}}{{jalink|Hikami District|Q5760344}}, small shrine). Tamba City, Hyogo Prefecture. Main enshrined deity, Hikono Tomomi ** Kawauchi Tatanuhi Shrine (Tamba Province {{jalink|Taki District|Q11431006}}, small shrine). Tamba City, Hyogo Prefecture. Main enshrined deity: Amaterasu Omikami
``Tadatanohi'' is a corruption of ``Tatenui,'' and it is said that it was originally a shrine of Tatebe that enshrined Hikonochi no Mikoto. * {{jalink|Specially Selected Names of Gods|Q11571092|Specially Selected Names of Gods}} states, ``If the shrine name of Tatenui is used now, it would be as if Hikosa Chi no Mikoto could be enshrined; In the article, ``Futunushi no Mikoto re-sewn the heavenly stone shield (Ame no Ihatate). Therefore, it is called ``Tatenui.'''' edited by Yukichi Takeda. Together with the editorial, it is written that the name ``Tatenui'' is not necessarily derived from ``Tatenui no Mikoto'' (Hikosatomo no Mikoto). === History === * {{jalink|Shinryo}} had over 53 towns, but in the first year of {{jalink|Kokoku|Q1143592}} (1340), it was invaded by Nobutsune, a subordinate of {{jalink|Koshifuyu|Q6455071}} of {{jalink|Northern Court (Japan)|Q1201819|Northern Court}}, and became without a living. * It is said that a wooden guardian dog (a cultural property designated by Miho Village) was dedicated to the for the {{jalink|Tenbun (era name)|Q1192853|Tenbun}} years (1532-1555). * There are records of reconstruction in {{jalink|Bunmei (Japan)|Q1193544|Bunmei}}8 (1476), {{jalink|Tensho|Q1145058}}17 (1589), and {{jalink|Kaei|Q1146125}}7 (1854). The current main hall is said to have been built in Tensho 17 and donated by Toshikatsu Kondo. * In early modern times, Shinta County Ichinomiya was called the shrine of more than 50 villages in the eastern half of Shinta County. * {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}}6 (1873), ranked as a prefectural company. * {{jalink|Showa|Q327567}}In 1965, the temple of worship was burnt down and ancient documents were lostJoyo Living. . === Nearby shrines === ; Tatenui Shrine : 1830 Shinta, Miho Village, Inashiki District, Ibaraki Prefecture (formerly Shinta Village, Shinta District) : Main enshrined deity, Kyotsu no Mikoto : Deities enshrined: {{jalink|Takamimusubi||Takōsei Reishin}}, {{jalink|Michizane Sugawara|Q382005|Michizane Sugawara}}, {{jalink|Honda Betsu no Mikoto}}, Takemikazuchi no Mikoto, {{jalink|Konohana Sakuyahime||Konohana Kaiyahime no Mikoto}}, Uka no Mitama no Mikoto :* The six deities enshrined are jointly enshrined deities (Kousanrei Shrine, Tenma Shrine, Katori Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Ninomiya Kashima Shrine, Sengen Shrine, and Uga Shrine). :* Founding unknown. According to the shrine's biography, it was founded in the {{jalink|Emperor Keiko|Q329723}} reign (unknown) and rebuilt in the first year (715) of {{jalink|Reiki|Q1373115}}Local history of Inashiki district. . Old {{jalink|Murasha|Q134917284}}. The shrines are Hijo-sha, Shikinai Tatenui-jinja Shrine, and Shinta-gun Sosha (framed). :* Located to the northeast of Shinta Castle Ruins, the area is also a candidate site for Shinta {{jalink|gunga|Q11643230}}. === Transportation === Take the JR Bus Kanto from JR Tsuchiura Station, get off at the "Gonaka" bus stop, and walk 10 minutes. === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist|2}} === References === * [https://www.ndl.go.jp/ National Diet Library]: Documents available for viewing from [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/ National Diet Library Digital Collection]. ** Nobuna Nakayama, Hiroshi Kurita, eds. “New edition of Hitachi Kokushi, Vol. 2.” Sekizenkan. Meiji 32-34 (1899-1901) ** Togo Yoshida, “Great Japanese Place Name Dictionary, Volume 2, 2nd Edition.” Toyamabo. October 17, 1907. ** "Meiji Shrine Archives Prefectural Gosha (Part 1)" edited by Meiji Shrine Archives Editorial Office. Meiji Shrine Materials Editorial Office. 1912 (Meiji 45) ** "Inashiki County Local History Honorary Book" edited by Izumi Hanawa. Religious newspaper company. Taisho 15 (1926). ** “Fudoki” edited by Yukichi Takeda, Iwanami Shoten, Iwanami Bunko. 1937. ** Iharaki Shimbun "Ibaraki Prefecture Shrine Photo Book". Iharaki Shimbunsha. 1942. * [http://www.bunkajoho.pref.ibaraki.jp/index_fudoki.html Ibaraki Prefecture Hitachi Province 1300th Anniversary]: [http://www.bunkajoho.pref.ibaraki.jp/fudoki/visit/32/index.html Tatenui Shrine]. Accessed March 20, 2017. * [http://www.joyoliving.co.jp/index.html Joyo Living]: [http://www.joyoliving.co.jp/special/heritage/00040/ Ibaraki History Walk "Guardian Dogs of Tatenui Shrine - Miura Village"]. May 20, 2009. Accessed November 15, 2013.{{Shinto horizontal}} {{List of shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tate-Nui Shin-Shiya}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tatenui Shrine (Miho, Ibaraki)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tatenui Shrine (Miho, Ibaraki)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Tatenui Shrine (Miho, Ibaraki)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:茨城県の神社]] [[Category:茨城県の旧県社]] [[Category:常陸国の式内社]] [[Category:美浦村の建築物]] [[Category:美浦村の歴史]] [[Category:一宮 (地域)]] [[Category:信太郡]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:kkkkkkkkkkkkkk]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qq]]

Oratory |Location = {{jalink|Ibaraki Prefecture|Q83273}}{{jalink|Inashiki District|Q1154673}}{{jalink|Miho Village|Q1203179}}Gochu 2988 |Position = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = Futoshi no Mikoto |Company rank = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small)
Old {{jalink|Prefectural company}} |Foundation = {{jalink|Emperor Suiko|Q298057}}16th year ({{jalink|608th year|Q31037}}) |Main shrine = |Alias = {{jalink|Shota County|Q11386034}}{{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q1656379}} |Fudasho, etc. = |Regular festival = {{jalink|May 15th|Q2562}} |Shinto ritual = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Ibaraki|label=Tatenui Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tatenui Shrine'' (Tatenui Shrine) is located at 2988 Gochu [http://www.oyashiro.or.jp/ According to the shrine register of the Ibaraki Prefectural Shrine Office, it is 2988 Kihara, Miura Village. (formerly {{jalink|Shota District|Q11386034}}{{jalink|Kihara Village|Q11518209}}) {{jalink|Shrine|Q845945}}. The old name was {{jalink|Shota-gun|Q11386034}}{{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q1656379}}. {{jalink|Shikiuchi-sha}} ({{jalink|Hitachi-kuni|Q1055353}} Shinta District, small shrine). Former {{jalink|kensha}}. There is also Tatenui Shrine with the same name in Shinta Miura Village. === Overview === [[File:Tatenui Shrine (Gochu, Miura Village, Ibaraki Prefecture) 03.jpg|thumb|First Torii]] [[File:Tatenui-jinja shrine Ni no Torii Gate (Miho village, Ibaraki prefecture).jpg|thumb|Ni no Torii Gate]] [[File:Tatenui Shrine (Gochu, Miura Village, Ibaraki Prefecture) 02.jpg|thumb|Chozusha]] The approach road faces the {{jalink|National Route 125|Q6156756}} existing road, but the shrine grounds are located in a secluded area and are difficult to find. A long approach path passes through a forest of giant trees, and the shrine building is located at the back. There are no shrine offices or prominent shrines within the precincts, so you can enjoy the simple composition of the grove of trees and shrine buildings.The precincts are designated by Ibaraki Prefecture {{jalink|natural environment conservation area|Q11612866}} (29, Ichinomiya). {{jalink|Hitachikuni Fudoki|Q11481687}} Fudu myth in the Shinta district sectionThe original text of Hitachikuni Fudoki is ``Takakiri, Koroi, Tenchi Kinkoshi, Plants and trees language no time, the name of the deity who descended from heaven, the title '''Futu Great God''', Junko Ashihara Nakatsukuni, Hehei Mountain River Arakyo no Rui, Great God Transformation Dojinbi, Mind Returns to Heaven, Immediate Zuishin''' Equipment Staff (commonly said, Ikawanogawa Mei), Armored Shield and Shogyokukei, All Take Off''', Detained Kageji, Immediately Riding Hakuun, Returning Soten. It is said to be the place where {{jalink|Ketsunushikami|Q1107286|Futsu Okami}} took off his shield, which is depicted in , and is therefore the sacred place of ``Tatenui''. There are two legends about the origin of the name Kihara, but both tell that the area has been a rich forest since ancient times. It has a deep relationship with {{jalink|Ami Shrine (Takeki, Ami Town)|Q17191825|Takekiami Shrine}} due to its association with the sacred place of Futsu mythology, and in ancient times it was called Ichinomiya Ninomiya as the main shrine for east and west Shinta District, and both {{jalink|Eiwa (Japan)|Q1145780|Eiwa}} 1st year (1375) of the Enmyin-ji Shrine and the Shrine and the Sobō Dojojo, which reads "Shonai Daiichi-no-Sobyo" Great Japanese Place Name Dictionary. . Tatenui Shrine was the Shinta District Ichinomiya Shrine, and was one of the most prestigious shrines in the former district area. An ancient ceremony (Kashima Shinto ritual) was also held in which the {{jalink|mikoshi|Q979940}} of Futsu no Mikoto was carried to {{jalink|Ami Shrine|Q16276818}} via Lake Kasumigaura. === Enshrined God === ; Main enshrined deity : Futsu no MikotoMeiji Shrine materials. (Futsusu no Kami, Futsusu Okami) ; Deity enshrined : {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Otomi Takamikoto}} Niza, {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Susanoo no Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Ukanomitama|Q308072 8|Ukatama no Mikoto}} Niza, Kosan Reimyo, {{jalink|Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|Ichikishima Hime no Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Kumano Taisha|Q11568887|Kumano Kaoroki no Mikoto}} * The precinct information board introduces the deeds of {{jalink|Futunushi||Ami Shrine}} in {{jalink|kiki|Q842308}}{{jalink|mythology|Q12827256}}, and considers the two deities to be the same deity (for Futsu Okami, please refer to {{jalink|Ami Shrine (Also see Takeki, Ami Town)|Q17191825|Takekuami Shrine}}). * There is also a separate legend that says the main enshrined deity is {{jalink|Hikosa Tomomi|Q135327097}}. * The merger due to shrine reorganization is below Ibaraki Prefecture Shrine Photo Book. . ** {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}}In 1941, merged with Itsukushima Shrine (Ichikishima Hime-no-Mikoto) in Sukazu, Oaza. ** In 1907, Hachiman Shrine ({{jalink|Honda Betsu no Mikoto}}) and Atago Shrine (Housan Reimyo Nisha) were merged into the precinct shrine Goryusha. ** In Meiji 45, Inari Shrine (Ugamitama no Mikoto), Orii Shrine ({{jalink|Yokamijin|Q644201|Koryu no Mikoto}}), and Itsukushima Shrine (Ichikishimahime no Mikoto) were merged into the precinct shrine Goryusha. === History === There is a company biography and a separate biography. In addition to the two theories about its founding, there is another theory that says it was built in 18 AD. . === Company biography === * Founded in {{jalink|Emperor Suiko|Q298057}}16 (608)Precinct information board. . * Searching for the auspicious origin of creation rituals in Fudu mythology. It is said that after the {{jalink|pacification of Ashihara China}}, Futsu Okami took off his armor at Kihara and ascended to heaven at Takakiri. Because he took off his armor, the place name ``Tatezu'' was born, and later became ``Tatenui.'' The place name ``Tatezuzan'' still remains on the shrine grounds. * The name of the village of Kihara is derived from {{jalink|Eisho|Q1193577}} 3rd year (1506), which was built in the precincts of a temple with a circumference of 5 jo, 8 shaku, and a height of 120 shaku.{{jalink |Sugi|Q147388|Cedar}} ({{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} withered in May 1868) and {{jalink|Kihara Castle (Hitachi Province)|Q11518195|Kihara Castle}}It was born by {{jalink|combined place name|Q11412460|combined}} of the ``Hara'' of the lord Kondo Shikibu Daisuke Fujiwara Toshikatsu (Kondo Toshikatsu). Until then, it was called Kamikoshi Village.Precinct information board, Meiji Shrine magazine materials, etc. According to the Miho Village Board of Education's explanation of the cultural property, the origin is a giant cedar tree with a circumference of more than 6 meters located in the north of the precincts. . === Separate story === * The new edition of Hitachi Kokushi states, ``He is said to be the enshrined deity {{jalink|Tomomichi Hikosa|Q135327097}}, the guardian of 33 villages in the Middle East of the district, and Ichinomiya of Sokuhon District (shaki, 28 shrines, 28 shrines summary origin) , this god was known as Amaterasu, the ancestor of {{jalink|Ibe}} of {{jalink|Kioku|Q864053}}, as well as {{jalink|Tegiho Negami}}, during the age of the gods. For the great god |Q455602}}, he built a magnificent palace and made various ritual vessels to serve, but since this god only sewed shields, {{jalink|shield sewing|Q11582552|shield sewing} }}It is also said to be a god (Nihongi, Kogo Shui) Even in the human era, there was a tatebe who made shields for ritual utensils.'' (original kana), and the shrine is said to enshrine Hikosa Tomomichi, the ``tatenui deity.'' * The name of the village of Kihara is said to be from an ancient legend that says, ``In the old ancient times, this place was a vast wasteland, with cedar and cypress trees growing here and there, hence the name Kihara.One day, an old man suddenly appeared and told the villagers, ``Your houses are very poor, and when you see this palace, you should build one.'' The old man went under the shade of a cedar tree, entered Tamahinu, followed the worship of the villagers, gathered in front of the shrine, and started his home in this village (28 shrines abbreviated engagement)' (original kana), and it is also said that the Kihara village was formed along with Tatenui Shrine. * In the local history of Inashiki District, there is a statement that says, ``Before the Meiji Restoration, there was a theory that people visited Tatenui Shrine and worshiped it, calling it the ancestral deity of carpenters,'' which suggests that a separate legend had spread in its own way until before the Meiji period. *Generally, the deity enshrined at the shrine called Tatenui Shrine is Hikono Tomomichi. In addition to our company, there were at least four other Tatenui Shrines or Tatebe Shikinai shrines, all of which were shrines of the ``Tatenui God'' (Hikono Chimei).** Tatenui Shrine ({{jalink|Tajima Province|Q1053727}}{{jalink|Yabu District (Hyogo Prefecture)|Q11666859|Yabu District}}, small shrine). Yabu City, Hyogo Prefecture. Main enshrined deity, Hikono Tomomi ** Tatenui Shrine (Tajima Province {{jalink|Keta District (Hyogo Prefecture)|Q21655271|Keta District}}, small shrine). Toyooka City, Hyogo Prefecture. Main enshrined deity, Hikono Tomomi ** Tatenui Shrine ({{jalink|Tamba Province|Q954511}}{{jalink|Hikami District|Q5760344}}, small shrine). Tamba City, Hyogo Prefecture. Main enshrined deity, Hikono Tomomi ** Kawauchi Tatanuhi Shrine (Tamba Province {{jalink|Taki District|Q11431006}}, small shrine). Tamba City, Hyogo Prefecture. Main enshrined deity: Amaterasu Omikami
``Tadatanohi'' is a corruption of ``Tatenui,'' and it is said that it was originally a shrine of Tatebe that enshrined Hikonochi no Mikoto. * {{jalink|Specially Selected Names of Gods|Q11571092|Specially Selected Names of Gods}} states, ``If the shrine name of Tatenui is used now, it would be as if Hikosa Chi no Mikoto could be enshrined; In the article, ``Futunushi no Mikoto re-sewn the heavenly stone shield (Ame no Ihatate). Therefore, it is called ``Tatenui.'''' edited by Yukichi Takeda. Together with the editorial, it is written that the name ``Tatenui'' is not necessarily derived from ``Tatenui no Mikoto'' (Hikosatomo no Mikoto). === History === * {{jalink|Shinryo}} had over 53 towns, but in the first year of {{jalink|Kokoku|Q1143592}} (1340), it was invaded by Nobutsune, a subordinate of {{jalink|Koshifuyu|Q6455071}} of {{jalink|Northern Court (Japan)|Q1201819|Northern Court}}, and became without a living. * It is said that a wooden guardian dog (a cultural property designated by Miho Village) was dedicated to the for the {{jalink|Tenbun (era name)|Q1192853|Tenbun}} years (1532-1555). * There are records of reconstruction in {{jalink|Bunmei (Japan)|Q1193544|Bunmei}}8 (1476), {{jalink|Tensho|Q1145058}}17 (1589), and {{jalink|Kaei|Q1146125}}7 (1854). The current main hall is said to have been built in Tensho 17 and donated by Toshikatsu Kondo. * In early modern times, Shinta County Ichinomiya was called the shrine of more than 50 villages in the eastern half of Shinta County. * {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}}6 (1873), ranked as a prefectural company. * {{jalink|Showa|Q327567}}In 1965, the temple of worship was burnt down and ancient documents were lostJoyo Living. . === Nearby shrines === ; Tatenui Shrine : 1830 Shinta, Miho Village, Inashiki District, Ibaraki Prefecture (formerly Shinta Village, Shinta District) : Main enshrined deity, Kyotsu no Mikoto : Deities enshrined: {{jalink|Takamimusubi||Takōsei Reishin}}, {{jalink|Michizane Sugawara|Q382005|Michizane Sugawara}}, {{jalink|Honda Betsu no Mikoto}}, Takemikazuchi no Mikoto, {{jalink|Konohana Sakuyahime||Konohana Kaiyahime no Mikoto}}, Uka no Mitama no Mikoto :* The six deities enshrined are jointly enshrined deities (Kousanrei Shrine, Tenma Shrine, Katori Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Ninomiya Kashima Shrine, Sengen Shrine, and Uga Shrine). :* Founding unknown. According to the shrine's biography, it was founded in the {{jalink|Emperor Keiko|Q329723}} reign (unknown) and rebuilt in the first year (715) of {{jalink|Reiki|Q1373115}}Local history of Inashiki district. . Old {{jalink|Murasha|Q134917284}}. The shrines are Hijo-sha, Shikinai Tatenui-jinja Shrine, and Shinta-gun Sosha (framed). :* Located to the northeast of Shinta Castle Ruins, the area is also a candidate site for Shinta {{jalink|gunga|Q11643230}}. === Transportation === Take the JR Bus Kanto from JR Tsuchiura Station, get off at the "Gonaka" bus stop, and walk 10 minutes. === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist|2}} === References === * [https://www.ndl.go.jp/ National Diet Library]: Documents available for viewing from [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/ National Diet Library Digital Collection]. ** Nobuna Nakayama, Hiroshi Kurita, eds. “New edition of Hitachi Kokushi, Vol. 2.” Sekizenkan. Oratory |Location = {{jalink|Ibaraki Prefecture|Q83273}}{{jalink|Inashiki District|Q1154673}}{{jalink|Miho Village|Q1203179}}Gochu 2988 |Position = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = Futoshi no Mikoto |Company rank = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small)
Old {{jalink|Prefectural company}} |Foundation = {{jalink|Emperor Suiko|Q298057}}16th year ({{jalink|608th year|Q31037}}) |Main shrine = |Alias = {{jalink|Shota County|Q11386034}}{{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q1656379}} |Fudasho, etc. = |Regular festival = {{jalink|May 15th|Q2562}} |Shinto ritual = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Ibaraki|label=Tatenui Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tatenui Shrine'' (Tatenui Shrine) is located at 2988 Gochu [http://www.oyashiro.or.jp/ According to the shrine register of the Ibaraki Prefectural Shrine Office, it is 2988 Kihara, Miura Village. (formerly {{jalink|Shota District|Q11386034}}{{jalink|Kihara Village|Q11518209}}) {{jalink|Shrine|Q845945}}. The old name was {{jalink|Shota-gun|Q11386034}}{{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q1656379}}. {{jalink|Shikiuchi-sha}} ({{jalink|Hitachi-kuni|Q1055353}} Shinta District, small shrine). Former {{jalink|kensha}}. There is also Tatenui Shrine with the same name in Shinta Miura Village. === Overview === [[File:Tatenui Shrine (Gochu, Miura Village, Ibaraki Prefecture) 03.jpg|thumb|First Torii]] [[File:Tatenui-jinja shrine Ni no Torii Gate (Miho village, Ibaraki prefecture).jpg|thumb|Ni no Torii Gate]] [[File:Tatenui Shrine (Gochu, Miura Village, Ibaraki Prefecture) 02.jpg|thumb|Chozusha]] The approach road faces the {{jalink|National Route 125|Q6156756}} existing road, but the shrine grounds are located in a secluded area and are difficult to find. A long approach path passes through a forest of giant trees, and the shrine building is located at the back. There are no shrine offices or prominent shrines within the precincts, so you can enjoy the simple composition of the grove of trees and shrine buildings.The precincts are designated by Ibaraki Prefecture {{jalink|natural environment conservation area|Q11612866}} (29, Ichinomiya). {{jalink|Hitachikuni Fudoki|Q11481687}} Fudu myth in the Shinta district sectionThe original text of Hitachikuni Fudoki is ``Takakiri, Koroi, Tenchi Kinkoshi, Plants and trees language no time, the name of the deity who descended from heaven, the title '''Futu Great God''', Junko Ashihara Nakatsukuni, Hehei Mountain River Arakyo no Rui, Great God Transformation Dojinbi, Mind Returns to Heaven, Immediate Zuishin''' Equipment Staff (commonly said, Ikawanogawa Mei), Armored Shield and Shogyokukei, All Take Off''', Detained Kageji, Immediately Riding Hakuun, Returning Soten. It is said to be the place where {{jalink|Ketsunushikami|Q1107286|Futsu Okami}} took off his shield, which is depicted in , and is therefore the sacred place of ``Tatenui''. There are two legends about the origin of the name Kihara, but both tell that the area has been a rich forest since ancient times. It has a deep relationship with {{jalink|Ami Shrine (Takeki, Ami Town)|Q17191825|Takekiami Shrine}} due to its association with the sacred place of Futsu mythology, and in ancient times it was called Ichinomiya Ninomiya as the main shrine for east and west Shinta District, and both {{jalink|Eiwa (Japan)|Q1145780|Eiwa}} 1st year (1375) of the Enmyin-ji Shrine and the Shrine and the Sobō Dojojo, which reads "Shonai Daiichi-no-Sobyo" Great Japanese Place Name Dictionary. . Tatenui Shrine was the Shinta District Ichinomiya Shrine, and was one of the most prestigious shrines in the former district area. An ancient ceremony (Kashima Shinto ritual) was also held in which the {{jalink|mikoshi|Q979940}} of Futsu no Mikoto was carried to {{jalink|Ami Shrine|Q16276818}} via Lake Kasumigaura. === Enshrined God === ; Main enshrined deity : Futsu no MikotoMeiji Shrine materials. (Futsusu no Kami, Futsusu Okami) ; Deity enshrined : {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Otomi Takamikoto}} Niza, {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Susanoo no Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Ukanomitama|Q308072 8|Ukatama no Mikoto}} Niza, Kosan Reimyo, {{jalink|Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|Ichikishima Hime no Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Kumano Taisha|Q11568887|Kumano Kaoroki no Mikoto}} * The precinct information board introduces the deeds of {{jalink|Futunushi||Ami Shrine}} in {{jalink|kiki|Q842308}}{{jalink|mythology|Q12827256}}, and considers the two deities to be the same deity (for Futsu Okami, please refer to {{jalink|Ami Shrine (Also see Takeki, Ami Town)|Q17191825|Takekuami Shrine}}). * There is also a separate legend that says the main enshrined deity is {{jalink|Hikosa Tomomi|Q135327097}}. * The merger due to shrine reorganization is below Ibaraki Prefecture Shrine Photo Book. . ** {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}}In 1941, merged with Itsukushima Shrine (Ichikishima Hime-no-Mikoto) in Sukazu, Oaza. ** In 1907, Hachiman Shrine ({{jalink|Honda Betsu no Mikoto}}) and Atago Shrine (Housan Reimyo Nisha) were merged into the precinct shrine Goryusha. ** In Meiji 45, Inari Shrine (Ugamitama no Mikoto), Orii Shrine ({{jalink|Yokamijin|Q644201|Koryu no Mikoto}}), and Itsukushima Shrine (Ichikishimahime no Mikoto) were merged into the precinct shrine Goryusha. === History === There is a company biography and a separate biography. In addition to the two theories about its founding, there is another theory that says it was built in 18 AD. . === Company biography === * Founded in {{jalink|Emperor Suiko|Q298057}}16 (608)Precinct information board. . * Searching for the auspicious origin of creation rituals in Fudu mythology. It is said that after the {{jalink|pacification of Ashihara China}}, Futsu Okami took off his armor at Kihara and ascended to heaven at Takakiri. Because he took off his armor, the place name ``Tatezu'' was born, and later became ``Tatenui.'' The place name ``Tatezuzan'' still remains on the shrine grounds. * The name of the village of Kihara is derived from {{jalink|Eisho|Q1193577}} 3rd year (1506), which was built in the precincts of a temple with a circumference of 5 jo, 8 shaku, and a height of 120 shaku.{{jalink |Sugi|Q147388|Cedar}} ({{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} withered in May 1868) and {{jalink|Kihara Castle (Hitachi Province)|Q11518195|Kihara Castle}}It was born by {{jalink|combined place name|Q11412460|combined}} of the ``Hara'' of the lord Kondo Shikibu Daisuke Fujiwara Toshikatsu (Kondo Toshikatsu). Until then, it was called Kamikoshi Village.Precinct information board, Meiji Shrine magazine materials, etc. According to the Miho Village Board of Education's explanation of the cultural property, the origin is a giant cedar tree with a circumference of more than 6 meters located in the north of the precincts. . === Separate story === * The new edition of Hitachi Kokushi states, ``He is said to be the enshrined deity {{jalink|Tomomichi Hikosa|Q135327097}}, the guardian of 33 villages in the Middle East of the district, and Ichinomiya of Sokuhon District (shaki, 28 shrines, 28 shrines summary origin) , this god was known as Amaterasu, the ancestor of {{jalink|Ibe}} of {{jalink|Kioku|Q864053}}, as well as {{jalink|Tegiho Negami}}, during the age of the gods. For the great god |Q455602}}, he built a magnificent palace and made various ritual vessels to serve, but since this god only sewed shields, {{jalink|shield sewing|Q11582552|shield sewing} }}It is also said to be a god (Nihongi, Kogo Shui) Even in the human era, there was a tatebe who made shields for ritual utensils.'' (original kana), and the shrine is said to enshrine Hikosa Tomomichi, the ``tatenui deity.'' * The name of the village of Kihara is said to be from an ancient legend that says, ``In the old ancient times, this place was a vast wasteland, with cedar and cypress trees growing here and there, hence the name Kihara.One day, an old man suddenly appeared and told the villagers, ``Your houses are very poor, and when you see this palace, you should build one.'' The old man went under the shade of a cedar tree, entered Tamahinu, followed the worship of the villagers, gathered in front of the shrine, and started his home in this village (28 shrines abbreviated engagement)' (original kana), and it is also said that the Kihara village was formed along with Tatenui Shrine. * In the local history of Inashiki District, there is a statement that says, ``Before the Meiji Restoration, there was a theory that people visited Tatenui Shrine and worshiped it, calling it the ancestral deity of carpenters,'' which suggests that a separate legend had spread in its own way until before the Meiji period. *Generally, the deity enshrined at the shrine called Tatenui Shrine is Hikono Tomomichi. In addition to our company, there were at least four other Tatenui Shrines or Tatebe Shikinai shrines, all of which were shrines of the ``Tatenui God'' (Hikono Chimei).** Tatenui Shrine ({{jalink|Tajima Province|Q1053727}}{{jalink|Yabu District (Hyogo Prefecture)|Q11666859|Yabu District}}, small shrine). Yabu City, Hyogo Prefecture. Main enshrined deity, Hikono Tomomi ** Tatenui Shrine (Tajima Province {{jalink|Keta District (Hyogo Prefecture)|Q21655271|Keta District}}, small shrine). Toyooka City, Hyogo Prefecture. Main enshrined deity, Hikono Tomomi ** Tatenui Shrine ({{jalink|Tamba Province|Q954511}}{{jalink|Hikami District|Q5760344}}, small shrine). Tamba City, Hyogo Prefecture. Main enshrined deity, Hikono Tomomi ** Kawauchi Tatanuhi Shrine (Tamba Province {{jalink|Taki District|Q11431006}}, small shrine). Tamba City, Hyogo Prefecture. Main enshrined deity: Amaterasu Omikami
``Tadatanohi'' is a corruption of ``Tatenui,'' and it is said that it was originally a shrine of Tatebe that enshrined Hikonochi no Mikoto. * {{jalink|Specially Selected Names of Gods|Q11571092|Specially Selected Names of Gods}} states, ``If the shrine name of Tatenui is used now, it would be as if Hikosa Chi no Mikoto could be enshrined; In the article, ``Futunushi no Mikoto re-sewn the heavenly stone shield (Ame no Ihatate). Therefore, it is called ``Tatenui.'''' edited by Yukichi Takeda. Together with the editorial, it is written that the name ``Tatenui'' is not necessarily derived from ``Tatenui no Mikoto'' (Hikosatomo no Mikoto). === History === * {{jalink|Shinryo}} had over 53 towns, but in the first year of {{jalink|Kokoku|Q1143592}} (1340), it was invaded by Nobutsune, a subordinate of {{jalink|Koshifuyu|Q6455071}} of {{jalink|Northern Court (Japan)|Q1201819|Northern Court}}, and became without a living. * It is said that a wooden guardian dog (a cultural property designated by Miho Village) was dedicated to the for the {{jalink|Tenbun (era name)|Q1192853|Tenbun}} years (1532-1555). * There are records of reconstruction in {{jalink|Bunmei (Japan)|Q1193544|Bunmei}}8 (1476), {{jalink|Tensho|Q1145058}}17 (1589), and {{jalink|Kaei|Q1146125}}7 (1854). The current main hall is said to have been built in Tensho 17 and donated by Toshikatsu Kondo. * In early modern times, Shinta County Ichinomiya was called the shrine of more than 50 villages in the eastern half of Shinta County. * {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}}6 (1873), ranked as a prefectural company. * {{jalink|Showa|Q327567}}In 1965, the temple of worship was burnt down and ancient documents were lostJoyo Living. . === Nearby shrines === ; Tatenui Shrine : 1830 Shinta, Miho Village, Inashiki District, Ibaraki Prefecture (formerly Shinta Village, Shinta District) : Main enshrined deity, Kyotsu no Mikoto : Deities enshrined: {{jalink|Takamimusubi||Takōsei Reishin}}, {{jalink|Michizane Sugawara|Q382005|Michizane Sugawara}}, {{jalink|Honda Betsu no Mikoto}}, Takemikazuchi no Mikoto, {{jalink|Konohana Sakuyahime||Konohana Kaiyahime no Mikoto}}, Uka no Mitama no Mikoto :* The six deities enshrined are jointly enshrined deities (Kousanrei Shrine, Tenma Shrine, Katori Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Ninomiya Kashima Shrine, Sengen Shrine, and Uga Shrine). :* Founding unknown. According to the shrine's biography, it was founded in the {{jalink|Emperor Keiko|Q329723}} reign (unknown) and rebuilt in the first year (715) of {{jalink|Reiki|Q1373115}}Local history of Inashiki district. . Old {{jalink|Murasha|Q134917284}}. The shrines are Hijo-sha, Shikinai Tatenui-jinja Shrine, and Shinta-gun Sosha (framed). :* Located to the northeast of Shinta Castle Ruins, the area is also a candidate site for Shinta {{jalink|gunga|Q11643230}}. === Transportation === Take the JR Bus Kanto from JR Tsuchiura Station, get off at the "Gonaka" bus stop, and walk 10 minutes. === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist|2}} === References === * [https://www.ndl.go.jp/ National Diet Library]: Documents available for viewing from [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/ National Diet Library Digital Collection]. ** Nobuna Nakayama, Hiroshi Kurita, eds. “New edition of Hitachi Kokushi, Vol. 2.” Sekizenkan. '茨城県稲敷郡美浦村郷中2988番地', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '茨城県稲敷郡美浦村木原', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Tatenui Shrine (Miho, Ibaraki)}} [[JA:楯縫神社 (美浦村郷中)]] {{wikidata link|Q17211507}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Tatenui Shrine |Image =[[File:Tatenui Shrine (Gochu, Miura Village, Ibaraki Prefecture) 01.jpg|250px|Tatenui Shrine (Miura Village Gochu)]]
Oratory |Location = {{jalink|Ibaraki Prefecture|Q83273}}{{jalink|Inashiki District|Q1154673}}{{jalink|Miho Village|Q1203179}}Gochu 2988 |Position = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = Futoshi no Mikoto |Company rank = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small)
Old {{jalink|Prefectural company}} |Foundation = {{jalink|Emperor Suiko|Q298057}}16th year ({{jalink|608th year|Q31037}}) |Main shrine = |Alias = {{jalink|Shota County|Q11386034}}{{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q1656379}} |Fudasho, etc. = |Regular festival = {{jalink|May 15th|Q2562}} |Shinto ritual = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Ibaraki|label=Tatenui Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tatenui Shrine'' (Tatenui Shrine) is located at 2988 Gochu [http://www.oyashiro.or.jp/ According to the shrine register of the Ibaraki Prefectural Shrine Office, it is 2988 Kihara, Miura Village. (formerly {{jalink|Shota District|Q11386034}}{{jalink|Kihara Village|Q11518209}}) {{jalink|Shrine|Q845945}}. The old name was {{jalink|Shota-gun|Q11386034}}{{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q1656379}}. {{jalink|Shikiuchi-sha}} ({{jalink|Hitachi-kuni|Q1055353}} Shinta District, small shrine). Former {{jalink|kensha}}. There is also Tatenui Shrine with the same name in Shinta Miura Village. === Overview === [[File:Tatenui Shrine (Gochu, Miura Village, Ibaraki Prefecture) 03.jpg|thumb|First Torii]] [[File:Tatenui-jinja shrine Ni no Torii Gate (Miho village, Ibaraki prefecture).jpg|thumb|Ni no Torii Gate]] [[File:Tatenui Shrine (Gochu, Miura Village, Ibaraki Prefecture) 02.jpg|thumb|Chozusha]] The approach road faces the {{jalink|National Route 125|Q6156756}} existing road, but the shrine grounds are located in a secluded area and are difficult to find. A long approach path passes through a forest of giant trees, and the shrine building is located at the back. There are no shrine offices or prominent shrines within the precincts, so you can enjoy the simple composition of the grove of trees and shrine buildings.The precincts are designated by Ibaraki Prefecture {{jalink|natural environment conservation area|Q11612866}} (29, Ichinomiya). {{jalink|Hitachikuni Fudoki|Q11481687}} Fudu myth in the Shinta district sectionThe original text of Hitachikuni Fudoki is ``Takakiri, Koroi, Tenchi Kinkoshi, Plants and trees language no time, the name of the deity who descended from heaven, the title '''Futu Great God''', Junko Ashihara Nakatsukuni, Hehei Mountain River Arakyo no Rui, Great God Transformation Dojinbi, Mind Returns to Heaven, Immediate Zuishin''' Equipment Staff (commonly said, Ikawanogawa Mei), Armored Shield and Shogyokukei, All Take Off''', Detained Kageji, Immediately Riding Hakuun, Returning Soten. It is said to be the place where {{jalink|Ketsunushikami|Q1107286|Futsu Okami}} took off his shield, which is depicted in , and is therefore the sacred place of ``Tatenui''. There are two legends about the origin of the name Kihara, but both tell that the area has been a rich forest since ancient times. It has a deep relationship with {{jalink|Ami Shrine (Takeki, Ami Town)|Q17191825|Takekiami Shrine}} due to its association with the sacred place of Futsu mythology, and in ancient times it was called Ichinomiya Ninomiya as the main shrine for east and west Shinta District, and both {{jalink|Eiwa (Japan)|Q1145780|Eiwa}} 1st year (1375) of the Enmyin-ji Shrine and the Shrine and the Sobō Dojojo, which reads "Shonai Daiichi-no-Sobyo" Great Japanese Place Name Dictionary. . Tatenui Shrine was the Shinta District Ichinomiya Shrine, and was one of the most prestigious shrines in the former district area. An ancient ceremony (Kashima Shinto ritual) was also held in which the {{jalink|mikoshi|Q979940}} of Futsu no Mikoto was carried to {{jalink|Ami Shrine|Q16276818}} via Lake Kasumigaura. === Enshrined God === ; Main enshrined deity : Futsu no MikotoMeiji Shrine materials. (Futsusu no Kami, Futsusu Okami) ; Deity enshrined : {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Otomi Takamikoto}} Niza, {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Susanoo no Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Ukanomitama|Q308072 8|Ukatama no Mikoto}} Niza, Kosan Reimyo, {{jalink|Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|Ichikishima Hime no Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Kumano Taisha|Q11568887|Kumano Kaoroki no Mikoto}} * The precinct information board introduces the deeds of {{jalink|Futunushi||Ami Shrine}} in {{jalink|kiki|Q842308}}{{jalink|mythology|Q12827256}}, and considers the two deities to be the same deity (for Futsu Okami, please refer to {{jalink|Ami Shrine (Also see Takeki, Ami Town)|Q17191825|Takekuami Shrine}}). * There is also a separate legend that says the main enshrined deity is {{jalink|Hikosa Tomomi|Q135327097}}. * The merger due to shrine reorganization is below Ibaraki Prefecture Shrine Photo Book. . ** {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}}In 1941, merged with Itsukushima Shrine (Ichikishima Hime-no-Mikoto) in Sukazu, Oaza. ** In 1907, Hachiman Shrine ({{jalink|Honda Betsu no Mikoto}}) and Atago Shrine (Housan Reimyo Nisha) were merged into the precinct shrine Goryusha. ** In Meiji 45, Inari Shrine (Ugamitama no Mikoto), Orii Shrine ({{jalink|Yokamijin|Q644201|Koryu no Mikoto}}), and Itsukushima Shrine (Ichikishimahime no Mikoto) were merged into the precinct shrine Goryusha. === History === There is a company biography and a separate biography. In addition to the two theories about its founding, there is another theory that says it was built in 18 AD. . === Company biography === * Founded in {{jalink|Emperor Suiko|Q298057}}16 (608)Precinct information board. . * Searching for the auspicious origin of creation rituals in Fudu mythology. It is said that after the {{jalink|pacification of Ashihara China}}, Futsu Okami took off his armor at Kihara and ascended to heaven at Takakiri. Because he took off his armor, the place name ``Tatezu'' was born, and later became ``Tatenui.'' The place name ``Tatezuzan'' still remains on the shrine grounds. * The name of the village of Kihara is derived from {{jalink|Eisho|Q1193577}} 3rd year (1506), which was built in the precincts of a temple with a circumference of 5 jo, 8 shaku, and a height of 120 shaku.{{jalink |Sugi|Q147388|Cedar}} ({{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} withered in May 1868) and {{jalink|Kihara Castle (Hitachi Province)|Q11518195|Kihara Castle}}It was born by {{jalink|combined place name|Q11412460|combined}} of the ``Hara'' of the lord Kondo Shikibu Daisuke Fujiwara Toshikatsu (Kondo Toshikatsu). Until then, it was called Kamikoshi Village.Precinct information board, Meiji Shrine magazine materials, etc. According to the Miho Village Board of Education's explanation of the cultural property, the origin is a giant cedar tree with a circumference of more than 6 meters located in the north of the precincts. . === Separate story === * The new edition of Hitachi Kokushi states, ``He is said to be the enshrined deity {{jalink|Tomomichi Hikosa|Q135327097}}, the guardian of 33 villages in the Middle East of the district, and Ichinomiya of Sokuhon District (shaki, 28 shrines, 28 shrines summary origin) , this god was known as Amaterasu, the ancestor of {{jalink|Ibe}} of {{jalink|Kioku|Q864053}}, as well as {{jalink|Tegiho Negami}}, during the age of the gods. For the great god |Q455602}}, he built a magnificent palace and made various ritual vessels to serve, but since this god only sewed shields, {{jalink|shield sewing|Q11582552|shield sewing} }}It is also said to be a god (Nihongi, Kogo Shui) Even in the human era, there was a tatebe who made shields for ritual utensils.'' (original kana), and the shrine is said to enshrine Hikosa Tomomichi, the ``tatenui deity.'' * The name of the village of Kihara is said to be from an ancient legend that says, ``In the old ancient times, this place was a vast wasteland, with cedar and cypress trees growing here and there, hence the name Kihara.One day, an old man suddenly appeared and told the villagers, ``Your houses are very poor, and when you see this palace, you should build one.'' The old man went under the shade of a cedar tree, entered Tamahinu, followed the worship of the villagers, gathered in front of the shrine, and started his home in this village (28 shrines abbreviated engagement)' (original kana), and it is also said that the Kihara village was formed along with Tatenui Shrine. * In the local history of Inashiki District, there is a statement that says, ``Before the Meiji Restoration, there was a theory that people visited Tatenui Shrine and worshiped it, calling it the ancestral deity of carpenters,'' which suggests that a separate legend had spread in its own way until before the Meiji period. *Generally, the deity enshrined at the shrine called Tatenui Shrine is Hikono Tomomichi. In addition to our company, there were at least four other Tatenui Shrines or Tatebe Shikinai shrines, all of which were shrines of the ``Tatenui God'' (Hikono Chimei).** Tatenui Shrine ({{jalink|Tajima Province|Q1053727}}{{jalink|Yabu District (Hyogo Prefecture)|Q11666859|Yabu District}}, small shrine). Yabu City, Hyogo Prefecture. Main enshrined deity, Hikono Tomomi ** Tatenui Shrine (Tajima Province {{jalink|Keta District (Hyogo Prefecture)|Q21655271|Keta District}}, small shrine). Toyooka City, Hyogo Prefecture. Main enshrined deity, Hikono Tomomi ** Tatenui Shrine ({{jalink|Tamba Province|Q954511}}{{jalink|Hikami District|Q5760344}}, small shrine). Tamba City, Hyogo Prefecture. Main enshrined deity, Hikono Tomomi ** Kawauchi Tatanuhi Shrine (Tamba Province {{jalink|Taki District|Q11431006}}, small shrine). Tamba City, Hyogo Prefecture. Main enshrined deity: Amaterasu Omikami
``Tadatanohi'' is a corruption of ``Tatenui,'' and it is said that it was originally a shrine of Tatebe that enshrined Hikonochi no Mikoto. * {{jalink|Specially Selected Names of Gods|Q11571092|Specially Selected Names of Gods}} states, ``If the shrine name of Tatenui is used now, it would be as if Hikosa Chi no Mikoto could be enshrined; In the article, ``Futunushi no Mikoto re-sewn the heavenly stone shield (Ame no Ihatate). Therefore, it is called ``Tatenui.'''' edited by Yukichi Takeda. Together with the editorial, it is written that the name ``Tatenui'' is not necessarily derived from ``Tatenui no Mikoto'' (Hikosatomo no Mikoto). === History === * {{jalink|Shinryo}} had over 53 towns, but in the first year of {{jalink|Kokoku|Q1143592}} (1340), it was invaded by Nobutsune, a subordinate of {{jalink|Koshifuyu|Q6455071}} of {{jalink|Northern Court (Japan)|Q1201819|Northern Court}}, and became without a living. * It is said that a wooden guardian dog (a cultural property designated by Miho Village) was dedicated to the for the {{jalink|Tenbun (era name)|Q1192853|Tenbun}} years (1532-1555). * There are records of reconstruction in {{jalink|Bunmei (Japan)|Q1193544|Bunmei}}8 (1476), {{jalink|Tensho|Q1145058}}17 (1589), and {{jalink|Kaei|Q1146125}}7 (1854). The current main hall is said to have been built in Tensho 17 and donated by Toshikatsu Kondo. * In early modern times, Shinta County Ichinomiya was called the shrine of more than 50 villages in the eastern half of Shinta County. * {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}}6 (1873), ranked as a prefectural company. * {{jalink|Showa|Q327567}}In 1965, the temple of worship was burnt down and ancient documents were lostJoyo Living. . === Nearby shrines === ; Tatenui Shrine : 1830 Shinta, Miho Village, Inashiki District, Ibaraki Prefecture (formerly Shinta Village, Shinta District) : Main enshrined deity, Kyotsu no Mikoto : Deities enshrined: {{jalink|Takamimusubi||Takōsei Reishin}}, {{jalink|Michizane Sugawara|Q382005|Michizane Sugawara}}, {{jalink|Honda Betsu no Mikoto}}, Takemikazuchi no Mikoto, {{jalink|Konohana Sakuyahime||Konohana Kaiyahime no Mikoto}}, Uka no Mitama no Mikoto :* The six deities enshrined are jointly enshrined deities (Kousanrei Shrine, Tenma Shrine, Katori Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Ninomiya Kashima Shrine, Sengen Shrine, and Uga Shrine). :* Founding unknown. According to the shrine's biography, it was founded in the {{jalink|Emperor Keiko|Q329723}} reign (unknown) and rebuilt in the first year (715) of {{jalink|Reiki|Q1373115}}Local history of Inashiki district. . Old {{jalink|Murasha|Q134917284}}. The shrines are Hijo-sha, Shikinai Tatenui-jinja Shrine, and Shinta-gun Sosha (framed). :* Located to the northeast of Shinta Castle Ruins, the area is also a candidate site for Shinta {{jalink|gunga|Q11643230}}. === Transportation === Take the JR Bus Kanto from JR Tsuchiura Station, get off at the "Gonaka" bus stop, and walk 10 minutes. === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist|2}} === References === * [https://www.ndl.go.jp/ National Diet Library]: Documents available for viewing from [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/ National Diet Library Digital Collection]. ** Nobuna Nakayama, Hiroshi Kurita, eds. “New edition of Hitachi Kokushi, Vol. 2.” Sekizenkan.
