Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Tatenui Shrine |Image =[[File:Tatenui Shrine (Gochu, Miura Village, Ibaraki Prefecture) 01.jpg|250px|Tatenui Shrine (Miura Village Gochu)]]
Oratory |Location = {{ill|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture|Q83273|WD=Q83273}}{{ill|lt=Inashiki District|Q1154673|WD=Q1154673}}{{ill|lt=Miho Village|Q1203179|WD=Q1203179}}Gochu 2988 |Position = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = Futoshi no Mikoto |Company rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
Old {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural company|1=UNKNOWN}} |Foundation = {{ill|lt=Emperor Suiko|Q298057|WD=Q298057}}16th year ({{ill|lt=608th year|Q31037|WD=Q31037}}) |Main shrine = |Alias = {{ill|lt=Shota County|Q11386034|WD=Q11386034}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379}} |Fudasho, etc. = |Regular festival = {{ill|lt=May 15th|Q2562|WD=Q2562}} |Shinto ritual = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Ibaraki|label=Tatenui Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tatenui Shrine'' (Tatenui Shrine) is located at 2988 Gochu [http://www.oyashiro.or.jp/ According to the shrine register of the Ibaraki Prefectural Shrine Office, it is 2988 Kihara, Miura Village. (formerly {{ill|lt=Shota District|Q11386034|WD=Q11386034}}{{ill|lt=Kihara Village|Q11518209|WD=Q11518209}}) {{ill|lt=Shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}}. The old name was {{ill|lt=Shota-gun|Q11386034|WD=Q11386034}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379}}. {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikiuchi-sha|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Hitachi-kuni|Q1055353|WD=Q1055353}} Shinta District, small shrine). Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=UNKNOWN}}. There is also Tatenui Shrine with the same name in Shinta Miura Village. === Overview === [[File:Tatenui Shrine (Gochu, Miura Village, Ibaraki Prefecture) 03.jpg|thumb|First Torii]] [[File:Tatenui-jinja shrine Ni no Torii Gate (Miho village, Ibaraki prefecture).jpg|thumb|Ni no Torii Gate]] [[File:Tatenui Shrine (Gochu, Miura Village, Ibaraki Prefecture) 02.jpg|thumb|Chozusha]] The approach road faces the {{ill|lt=National Route 125|Q6156756|WD=Q6156756}} existing road, but the shrine grounds are located in a secluded area and are difficult to find. A long approach path passes through a forest of giant trees, and the shrine building is located at the back. There are no shrine offices or prominent shrines within the precincts, so you can enjoy the simple composition of the grove of trees and shrine buildings.The precincts are designated by Ibaraki Prefecture {{ill|lt=natural environment conservation area|Q11612866|WD=Q11612866}} (29, Ichinomiya). {{ill|lt=Hitachikuni Fudoki|Q11481687|WD=Q11481687}} Fudu myth in the Shinta district sectionThe original text of Hitachikuni Fudoki is ``Takakiri, Koroi, Tenchi Kinkoshi, Plants and trees language no time, the name of the deity who descended from heaven, the title '''Futu Great God''', Junko Ashihara Nakatsukuni, Hehei Mountain River Arakyo no Rui, Great God Transformation Dojinbi, Mind Returns to Heaven, Immediate Zuishin''' Equipment Staff (commonly said, Ikawanogawa Mei), Armored Shield and Shogyokukei, All Take Off''', Detained Kageji, Immediately Riding Hakuun, Returning Soten. It is said to be the place where {{ill|lt=Ketsunushikami|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsu Okami}} took off his shield, which is depicted in , and is therefore the sacred place of ``Tatenui''. There are two legends about the origin of the name Kihara, but both tell that the area has been a rich forest since ancient times. It has a deep relationship with {{ill|lt=Ami Shrine (Takeki, Ami Town)|Q17191825|WD=Q17191825|lt=Takekiami Shrine}} due to its association with the sacred place of Futsu mythology, and in ancient times it was called Ichinomiya Ninomiya as the main shrine for east and west Shinta District, and both {{ill|lt=Eiwa (Japan)|Q1145780|WD=Q1145780|lt=Eiwa}} 1st year (1375) of the Enmyin-ji Shrine and the Shrine and the Sobō Dojojo, which reads "Shonai Daiichi-no-Sobyo" Great Japanese Place Name Dictionary. . Tatenui Shrine was the Shinta District Ichinomiya Shrine, and was one of the most prestigious shrines in the former district area. An ancient ceremony (Kashima Shinto ritual) was also held in which the {{ill|lt=mikoshi|Q979940|WD=Q979940}} of Futsu no Mikoto was carried to {{ill|lt=Ami Shrine|Q16276818|WD=Q16276818}} via Lake Kasumigaura. === Enshrined God === ; Main enshrined deity : Futsu no MikotoMeiji Shrine materials. (Futsusu no Kami, Futsusu Okami) ; Deity enshrined : {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Otomi Takamikoto}} Niza, {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo no Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Ukanomitama|Q308072 8|WD=Q308072 8|lt=Ukatama no Mikoto}} Niza, Kosan Reimyo, {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishima Hime no Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kumano Taisha|Q11568887|WD=Q11568887|lt=Kumano Kaoroki no Mikoto}} * The precinct information board introduces the deeds of {{ill|lt=Futunushi|UNKNOWN|lt=Ami Shrine}} in {{ill|lt=kiki|Q842308|WD=Q842308}}{{ill|lt=mythology|Q12827256|WD=Q12827256}}, and considers the two deities to be the same deity (for Futsu Okami, please refer to {{ill|lt=Ami Shrine (Also see Takeki, Ami Town)|Q17191825|WD=Q17191825|lt=Takekuami Shrine}}). * There is also a separate legend that says the main enshrined deity is {{ill|lt=Hikosa Tomomi|Q135327097|WD=Q135327097}}. * The merger due to shrine reorganization is below Ibaraki Prefecture Shrine Photo Book. . ** {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}}In 1941, merged with Itsukushima Shrine (Ichikishima Hime-no-Mikoto) in Sukazu, Oaza. ** In 1907, Hachiman Shrine ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Honda Betsu no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}) and Atago Shrine (Housan Reimyo Nisha) were merged into the precinct shrine Goryusha. ** In Meiji 45, Inari Shrine (Ugamitama no Mikoto), Orii Shrine ({{ill|lt=Yokamijin|Q644201|WD=Q644201|lt=Koryu no Mikoto}}), and Itsukushima Shrine (Ichikishimahime no Mikoto) were merged into the precinct shrine Goryusha. === History === There is a company biography and a separate biography. In addition to the two theories about its founding, there is another theory that says it was built in 18 AD. . === Company biography === * Founded in {{ill|lt=Emperor Suiko|Q298057|WD=Q298057}}16 (608)Precinct information board. . * Searching for the auspicious origin of creation rituals in Fudu mythology. It is said that after the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=pacification of Ashihara China|1=UNKNOWN}}, Futsu Okami took off his armor at Kihara and ascended to heaven at Takakiri. Because he took off his armor, the place name ``Tatezu'' was born, and later became ``Tatenui.'' The place name ``Tatezuzan'' still remains on the shrine grounds. * The name of the village of Kihara is derived from {{ill|lt=Eisho|Q1193577|WD=Q1193577}} 3rd year (1506), which was built in the precincts of a temple with a circumference of 5 jo, 8 shaku, and a height of 120 shaku.{{jalink |Sugi|Q147388|Cedar}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} withered in May 1868) and {{ill|lt=Kihara Castle (Hitachi Province)|Q11518195|WD=Q11518195|lt=Kihara Castle}}It was born by {{ill|lt=combined place name|Q11412460|WD=Q11412460|lt=combined}} of the ``Hara'' of the lord Kondo Shikibu Daisuke Fujiwara Toshikatsu (Kondo Toshikatsu). Until then, it was called Kamikoshi Village.Precinct information board, Meiji Shrine magazine materials, etc. According to the Miho Village Board of Education's explanation of the cultural property, the origin is a giant cedar tree with a circumference of more than 6 meters located in the north of the precincts. . === Separate story === * The new edition of Hitachi Kokushi states, ``He is said to be the enshrined deity {{ill|lt=Tomomichi Hikosa|Q135327097|WD=Q135327097}}, the guardian of 33 villages in the Middle East of the district, and Ichinomiya of Sokuhon District (shaki, 28 shrines, 28 shrines summary origin) , this god was known as Amaterasu, the ancestor of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ibe|1=UNKNOWN}} of {{ill|lt=Kioku|Q864053|WD=Q864053}}, as well as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tegiho Negami|1=UNKNOWN}}, during the age of the gods. For the great god |Q455602}}, he built a magnificent palace and made various ritual vessels to serve, but since this god only sewed shields, {{jalink|shield sewing|Q11582552|shield sewing} }}It is also said to be a god (Nihongi, Kogo Shui) Even in the human era, there was a tatebe who made shields for ritual utensils.'' (original kana), and the shrine is said to enshrine Hikosa Tomomichi, the ``tatenui deity.'' * The name of the village of Kihara is said to be from an ancient legend that says, ``In the old ancient times, this place was a vast wasteland, with cedar and cypress trees growing here and there, hence the name Kihara.One day, an old man suddenly appeared and told the villagers, ``Your houses are very poor, and when you see this palace, you should build one.'' The old man went under the shade of a cedar tree, entered Tamahinu, followed the worship of the villagers, gathered in front of the shrine, and started his home in this village (28 shrines abbreviated engagement)' (original kana), and it is also said that the Kihara village was formed along with Tatenui Shrine. * In the local history of Inashiki District, there is a statement that says, ``Before the Meiji Restoration, there was a theory that people visited Tatenui Shrine and worshiped it, calling it the ancestral deity of carpenters,'' which suggests that a separate legend had spread in its own way until before the Meiji period. *Generally, the deity enshrined at the shrine called Tatenui Shrine is Hikono Tomomichi. In addition to our company, there were at least four other Tatenui Shrines or Tatebe Shikinai shrines, all of which were shrines of the ``Tatenui God'' (Hikono Chimei).** Tatenui Shrine ({{ill|lt=Tajima Province|Q1053727|WD=Q1053727}}{{ill|lt=Yabu District (Hyogo Prefecture)|Q11666859|WD=Q11666859|lt=Yabu District}}, small shrine). Yabu City, Hyogo Prefecture. Main enshrined deity, Hikono Tomomi ** Tatenui Shrine (Tajima Province {{ill|lt=Keta District (Hyogo Prefecture)|Q21655271|WD=Q21655271|lt=Keta District}}, small shrine). Toyooka City, Hyogo Prefecture. Main enshrined deity, Hikono Tomomi ** Tatenui Shrine ({{ill|lt=Tamba Province|Q954511|WD=Q954511}}{{ill|lt=Hikami District|Q5760344|WD=Q5760344}}, small shrine). Tamba City, Hyogo Prefecture. Main enshrined deity, Hikono Tomomi ** Kawauchi Tatanuhi Shrine (Tamba Province {{ill|lt=Taki District|Q11431006|WD=Q11431006}}, small shrine). Tamba City, Hyogo Prefecture. Main enshrined deity: Amaterasu Omikami
``Tadatanohi'' is a corruption of ``Tatenui,'' and it is said that it was originally a shrine of Tatebe that enshrined Hikonochi no Mikoto. * {{ill|lt=Specially Selected Names of Gods|Q11571092|WD=Q11571092|lt=Specially Selected Names of Gods}} states, ``If the shrine name of Tatenui is used now, it would be as if Hikosa Chi no Mikoto could be enshrined; In the article, ``Futunushi no Mikoto re-sewn the heavenly stone shield (Ame no Ihatate). Therefore, it is called ``Tatenui.'''' edited by Yukichi Takeda. Together with the editorial, it is written that the name ``Tatenui'' is not necessarily derived from ``Tatenui no Mikoto'' (Hikosatomo no Mikoto). === History === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinryo|1=UNKNOWN}} had over 53 towns, but in the first year of {{ill|lt=Kokoku|Q1143592|WD=Q1143592}} (1340), it was invaded by Nobutsune, a subordinate of {{ill|lt=Koshifuyu|Q6455071|WD=Q6455071}} of {{ill|lt=Northern Court (Japan)|Q1201819|WD=Q1201819|lt=Northern Court}}, and became without a living. * It is said that a wooden guardian dog (a cultural property designated by Miho Village) was dedicated to the for the {{ill|lt=Tenbun (era name)|Q1192853|WD=Q1192853|lt=Tenbun}} years (1532-1555). * There are records of reconstruction in {{ill|lt=Bunmei (Japan)|Q1193544|WD=Q1193544|lt=Bunmei}}8 (1476), {{ill|lt=Tensho|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}}17 (1589), and {{ill|lt=Kaei|Q1146125|WD=Q1146125}}7 (1854). The current main hall is said to have been built in Tensho 17 and donated by Toshikatsu Kondo. * In early modern times, Shinta County Ichinomiya was called the shrine of more than 50 villages in the eastern half of Shinta County. * {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}}6 (1873), ranked as a prefectural company. * {{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}}In 1965, the temple of worship was burnt down and ancient documents were lostJoyo Living. . === Nearby shrines === ; Tatenui Shrine : 1830 Shinta, Miho Village, Inashiki District, Ibaraki Prefecture (formerly Shinta Village, Shinta District) : Main enshrined deity, Kyotsu no Mikoto : Deities enshrined: {{ill|lt=Takamimusubi|UNKNOWN|lt=Takōsei Reishin}}, {{ill|lt=Michizane Sugawara|Q382005|WD=Q382005|lt=Michizane Sugawara}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Honda Betsu no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, Takemikazuchi no Mikoto, {{ill|lt=Konohana Sakuyahime|UNKNOWN|lt=Konohana Kaiyahime no Mikoto}}, Uka no Mitama no Mikoto :* The six deities enshrined are jointly enshrined deities (Kousanrei Shrine, Tenma Shrine, Katori Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Ninomiya Kashima Shrine, Sengen Shrine, and Uga Shrine). :* Founding unknown. According to the shrine's biography, it was founded in the {{ill|lt=Emperor Keiko|Q329723|WD=Q329723}} reign (unknown) and rebuilt in the first year (715) of {{ill|lt=Reiki|Q1373115|WD=Q1373115}}Local history of Inashiki district. . Old {{ill|lt=Murasha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. The shrines are Hijo-sha, Shikinai Tatenui-jinja Shrine, and Shinta-gun Sosha (framed). :* Located to the northeast of Shinta Castle Ruins, the area is also a candidate site for Shinta {{ill|lt=gunga|Q11643230|WD=Q11643230}}. === Transportation === Take the JR Bus Kanto from JR Tsuchiura Station, get off at the "Gonaka" bus stop, and walk 10 minutes. === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist|2}} === References === * [https://www.ndl.go.jp/ National Diet Library]: Documents available for viewing from [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/ National Diet Library Digital Collection]. ** Nobuna Nakayama, Hiroshi Kurita, eds. “New edition of Hitachi Kokushi, Vol. 2.” Sekizenkan. Oratory |Location = {{ill|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture|Q83273|WD=Q83273}}{{ill|lt=Inashiki District|Q1154673|WD=Q1154673}}{{ill|lt=Miho Village|Q1203179|WD=Q1203179}}Gochu 2988 |Position = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = Futoshi no Mikoto |Company rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
Old {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural company|1=UNKNOWN}} |Foundation = {{ill|lt=Emperor Suiko|Q298057|WD=Q298057}}16th year ({{ill|lt=608th year|Q31037|WD=Q31037}}) |Main shrine = |Alias = {{ill|lt=Shota County|Q11386034|WD=Q11386034}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379}} |Fudasho, etc. = |Regular festival = {{ill|lt=May 15th|Q2562|WD=Q2562}} |Shinto ritual = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Ibaraki|label=Tatenui Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tatenui Shrine'' (Tatenui Shrine) is located at 2988 Gochu [http://www.oyashiro.or.jp/ According to the shrine register of the Ibaraki Prefectural Shrine Office, it is 2988 Kihara, Miura Village. (formerly {{ill|lt=Shota District|Q11386034|WD=Q11386034}}{{ill|lt=Kihara Village|Q11518209|WD=Q11518209}}) {{ill|lt=Shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}}. The old name was {{ill|lt=Shota-gun|Q11386034|WD=Q11386034}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379}}. {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikiuchi-sha|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Hitachi-kuni|Q1055353|WD=Q1055353}} Shinta District, small shrine). Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226}}. There is also Tatenui Shrine with the same name in Shinta Miura Village. === Overview === [[File:Tatenui Shrine (Gochu, Miura Village, Ibaraki Prefecture) 03.jpg|thumb|First Torii]] [[File:Tatenui-jinja shrine Ni no Torii Gate (Miho village, Ibaraki prefecture).jpg|thumb|Ni no Torii Gate]] [[File:Tatenui Shrine (Gochu, Miura Village, Ibaraki Prefecture) 02.jpg|thumb|Chozusha]] The approach road faces the {{ill|lt=National Route 125|Q6156756|WD=Q6156756}} existing road, but the shrine grounds are located in a secluded area and are difficult to find. A long approach path passes through a forest of giant trees, and the shrine building is located at the back. There are no shrine offices or prominent shrines within the precincts, so you can enjoy the simple composition of the grove of trees and shrine buildings.The precincts are designated by Ibaraki Prefecture {{ill|lt=natural environment conservation area|Q11612866|WD=Q11612866}} (29, Ichinomiya). {{ill|lt=Hitachikuni Fudoki|Q11481687|WD=Q11481687}} Fudu myth in the Shinta district sectionThe original text of Hitachikuni Fudoki is ``Takakiri, Koroi, Tenchi Kinkoshi, Plants and trees language no time, the name of the deity who descended from heaven, the title '''Futu Great God''', Junko Ashihara Nakatsukuni, Hehei Mountain River Arakyo no Rui, Great God Transformation Dojinbi, Mind Returns to Heaven, Immediate Zuishin''' Equipment Staff (commonly said, Ikawanogawa Mei), Armored Shield and Shogyokukei, All Take Off''', Detained Kageji, Immediately Riding Hakuun, Returning Soten. It is said to be the place where {{ill|lt=Ketsunushikami|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsu Okami}} took off his shield, which is depicted in , and is therefore the sacred place of ``Tatenui''. There are two legends about the origin of the name Kihara, but both tell that the area has been a rich forest since ancient times. It has a deep relationship with {{ill|lt=Ami Shrine (Takeki, Ami Town)|Q17191825|WD=Q17191825|lt=Takekiami Shrine}} due to its association with the sacred place of Futsu mythology, and in ancient times it was called Ichinomiya Ninomiya as the main shrine for east and west Shinta District, and both {{ill|lt=Eiwa (Japan)|Q1145780|WD=Q1145780|lt=Eiwa}} 1st year (1375) of the Enmyin-ji Shrine and the Shrine and the Sobō Dojojo, which reads "Shonai Daiichi-no-Sobyo" Great Japanese Place Name Dictionary. . Tatenui Shrine was the Shinta District Ichinomiya Shrine, and was one of the most prestigious shrines in the former district area. An ancient ceremony (Kashima Shinto ritual) was also held in which the {{ill|lt=mikoshi|Q979940|WD=Q979940}} of Futsu no Mikoto was carried to {{ill|lt=Ami Shrine|Q16276818|WD=Q16276818}} via Lake Kasumigaura. === Enshrined God === ; Main enshrined deity : Futsu no MikotoMeiji Shrine materials. (Futsusu no Kami, Futsusu Okami) ; Deity enshrined : {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Otomi Takamikoto}} Niza, {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo no Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Ukanomitama|Q308072 8|WD=Q308072 8|lt=Ukatama no Mikoto}} Niza, Kosan Reimyo, {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishima Hime no Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kumano Taisha|Q11568887|WD=Q11568887|lt=Kumano Kaoroki no Mikoto}} * The precinct information board introduces the deeds of {{ill|lt=Futunushi|UNKNOWN|lt=Ami Shrine}} in {{ill|lt=kiki|Q842308|WD=Q842308}}{{ill|lt=mythology|Q12827256|WD=Q12827256}}, and considers the two deities to be the same deity (for Futsu Okami, please refer to {{ill|lt=Ami Shrine (Also see Takeki, Ami Town)|Q17191825|WD=Q17191825|lt=Takekuami Shrine}}). * There is also a separate legend that says the main enshrined deity is {{ill|lt=Hikosa Tomomi|Q135327097|WD=Q135327097}}. * The merger due to shrine reorganization is below Ibaraki Prefecture Shrine Photo Book. . ** {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}}In 1941, merged with Itsukushima Shrine (Ichikishima Hime-no-Mikoto) in Sukazu, Oaza. ** In 1907, Hachiman Shrine ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Honda Betsu no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}) and Atago Shrine (Housan Reimyo Nisha) were merged into the precinct shrine Goryusha. ** In Meiji 45, Inari Shrine (Ugamitama no Mikoto), Orii Shrine ({{ill|lt=Yokamijin|Q644201|WD=Q644201|lt=Koryu no Mikoto}}), and Itsukushima Shrine (Ichikishimahime no Mikoto) were merged into the precinct shrine Goryusha. === History === There is a company biography and a separate biography. In addition to the two theories about its founding, there is another theory that says it was built in 18 AD. . === Company biography === * Founded in {{ill|lt=Emperor Suiko|Q298057|WD=Q298057}}16 (608)Precinct information board. . * Searching for the auspicious origin of creation rituals in Fudu mythology. It is said that after the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=pacification of Ashihara China|1=UNKNOWN}}, Futsu Okami took off his armor at Kihara and ascended to heaven at Takakiri. Because he took off his armor, the place name ``Tatezu'' was born, and later became ``Tatenui.'' The place name ``Tatezuzan'' still remains on the shrine grounds. * The name of the village of Kihara is derived from {{ill|lt=Eisho|Q1193577|WD=Q1193577}} 3rd year (1506), which was built in the precincts of a temple with a circumference of 5 jo, 8 shaku, and a height of 120 shaku.{{jalink |Sugi|Q147388|Cedar}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} withered in May 1868) and {{ill|lt=Kihara Castle (Hitachi Province)|Q11518195|WD=Q11518195|lt=Kihara Castle}}It was born by {{ill|lt=combined place name|Q11412460|WD=Q11412460|lt=combined}} of the ``Hara'' of the lord Kondo Shikibu Daisuke Fujiwara Toshikatsu (Kondo Toshikatsu). Until then, it was called Kamikoshi Village.Precinct information board, Meiji Shrine magazine materials, etc. According to the Miho Village Board of Education's explanation of the cultural property, the origin is a giant cedar tree with a circumference of more than 6 meters located in the north of the precincts. . === Separate story === * The new edition of Hitachi Kokushi states, ``He is said to be the enshrined deity {{ill|lt=Tomomichi Hikosa|Q135327097|WD=Q135327097}}, the guardian of 33 villages in the Middle East of the district, and Ichinomiya of Sokuhon District (shaki, 28 shrines, 28 shrines summary origin) , this god was known as Amaterasu, the ancestor of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ibe|1=UNKNOWN}} of {{ill|lt=Kioku|Q864053|WD=Q864053}}, as well as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tegiho Negami|1=UNKNOWN}}, during the age of the gods. For the great god |Q455602}}, he built a magnificent palace and made various ritual vessels to serve, but since this god only sewed shields, {{jalink|shield sewing|Q11582552|shield sewing} }}It is also said to be a god (Nihongi, Kogo Shui) Even in the human era, there was a tatebe who made shields for ritual utensils.'' (original kana), and the shrine is said to enshrine Hikosa Tomomichi, the ``tatenui deity.'' * The name of the village of Kihara is said to be from an ancient legend that says, ``In the old ancient times, this place was a vast wasteland, with cedar and cypress trees growing here and there, hence the name Kihara.One day, an old man suddenly appeared and told the villagers, ``Your houses are very poor, and when you see this palace, you should build one.'' The old man went under the shade of a cedar tree, entered Tamahinu, followed the worship of the villagers, gathered in front of the shrine, and started his home in this village (28 shrines abbreviated engagement)' (original kana), and it is also said that the Kihara village was formed along with Tatenui Shrine. * In the local history of Inashiki District, there is a statement that says, ``Before the Meiji Restoration, there was a theory that people visited Tatenui Shrine and worshiped it, calling it the ancestral deity of carpenters,'' which suggests that a separate legend had spread in its own way until before the Meiji period. *Generally, the deity enshrined at the shrine called Tatenui Shrine is Hikono Tomomichi. In addition to our company, there were at least four other Tatenui Shrines or Tatebe Shikinai shrines, all of which were shrines of the ``Tatenui God'' (Hikono Chimei).** Tatenui Shrine ({{ill|lt=Tajima Province|Q1053727|WD=Q1053727}}{{ill|lt=Yabu District (Hyogo Prefecture)|Q11666859|WD=Q11666859|lt=Yabu District}}, small shrine). Yabu City, Hyogo Prefecture. Main enshrined deity, Hikono Tomomi ** Tatenui Shrine (Tajima Province {{ill|lt=Keta District (Hyogo Prefecture)|Q21655271|WD=Q21655271|lt=Keta District}}, small shrine). Toyooka City, Hyogo Prefecture. Main enshrined deity, Hikono Tomomi ** Tatenui Shrine ({{ill|lt=Tamba Province|Q954511|WD=Q954511}}{{ill|lt=Hikami District|Q5760344|WD=Q5760344}}, small shrine). Tamba City, Hyogo Prefecture. Main enshrined deity, Hikono Tomomi ** Kawauchi Tatanuhi Shrine (Tamba Province {{ill|lt=Taki District|Q11431006|WD=Q11431006}}, small shrine). Tamba City, Hyogo Prefecture. Main enshrined deity: Amaterasu Omikami
``Tadatanohi'' is a corruption of ``Tatenui,'' and it is said that it was originally a shrine of Tatebe that enshrined Hikonochi no Mikoto. * {{ill|lt=Specially Selected Names of Gods|Q11571092|WD=Q11571092|lt=Specially Selected Names of Gods}} states, ``If the shrine name of Tatenui is used now, it would be as if Hikosa Chi no Mikoto could be enshrined; In the article, ``Futunushi no Mikoto re-sewn the heavenly stone shield (Ame no Ihatate). Therefore, it is called ``Tatenui.'''' edited by Yukichi Takeda. Together with the editorial, it is written that the name ``Tatenui'' is not necessarily derived from ``Tatenui no Mikoto'' (Hikosatomo no Mikoto). === History === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinryo|1=UNKNOWN}} had over 53 towns, but in the first year of {{ill|lt=Kokoku|Q1143592|WD=Q1143592}} (1340), it was invaded by Nobutsune, a subordinate of {{ill|lt=Koshifuyu|Q6455071|WD=Q6455071}} of {{ill|lt=Northern Court (Japan)|Q1201819|WD=Q1201819|lt=Northern Court}}, and became without a living. * It is said that a wooden guardian dog (a cultural property designated by Miho Village) was dedicated to the for the {{ill|lt=Tenbun (era name)|Q1192853|WD=Q1192853|lt=Tenbun}} years (1532-1555). * There are records of reconstruction in {{ill|lt=Bunmei (Japan)|Q1193544|WD=Q1193544|lt=Bunmei}}8 (1476), {{ill|lt=Tensho|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}}17 (1589), and {{ill|lt=Kaei|Q1146125|WD=Q1146125}}7 (1854). The current main hall is said to have been built in Tensho 17 and donated by Toshikatsu Kondo. * In early modern times, Shinta County Ichinomiya was called the shrine of more than 50 villages in the eastern half of Shinta County. * {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}}6 (1873), ranked as a prefectural company. * {{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}}In 1965, the temple of worship was burnt down and ancient documents were lostJoyo Living. . === Nearby shrines === ; Tatenui Shrine : 1830 Shinta, Miho Village, Inashiki District, Ibaraki Prefecture (formerly Shinta Village, Shinta District) : Main enshrined deity, Kyotsu no Mikoto : Deities enshrined: {{ill|lt=Takamimusubi|UNKNOWN|lt=Takōsei Reishin}}, {{ill|lt=Michizane Sugawara|Q382005|WD=Q382005|lt=Michizane Sugawara}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Honda Betsu no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, Takemikazuchi no Mikoto, {{ill|lt=Konohana Sakuyahime|UNKNOWN|lt=Konohana Kaiyahime no Mikoto}}, Uka no Mitama no Mikoto :* The six deities enshrined are jointly enshrined deities (Kousanrei Shrine, Tenma Shrine, Katori Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Ninomiya Kashima Shrine, Sengen Shrine, and Uga Shrine). :* Founding unknown. According to the shrine's biography, it was founded in the {{ill|lt=Emperor Keiko|Q329723|WD=Q329723}} reign (unknown) and rebuilt in the first year (715) of {{ill|lt=Reiki|Q1373115|WD=Q1373115}}Local history of Inashiki district. . Old {{ill|lt=Murasha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. The shrines are Hijo-sha, Shikinai Tatenui-jinja Shrine, and Shinta-gun Sosha (framed). :* Located to the northeast of Shinta Castle Ruins, the area is also a candidate site for Shinta {{ill|lt=gunga|Q11643230|WD=Q11643230}}. === Transportation === Take the JR Bus Kanto from JR Tsuchiura Station, get off at the "Gonaka" bus stop, and walk 10 minutes. === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist|2}} === References === * [https://www.ndl.go.jp/ National Diet Library]: Documents available for viewing from [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/ National Diet Library Digital Collection]. ** Nobuna Nakayama, Hiroshi Kurita, eds. “New edition of Hitachi Kokushi, Vol. 2.” Sekizenkan. Oratory |Location = {{ill|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture|Q83273|WD=Q83273}}{{ill|lt=Inashiki District|Q1154673|WD=Q1154673}}{{ill|lt=Miho Village|Q1203179|WD=Q1203179}}Gochu 2988 |Position = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = Futoshi no Mikoto |Company rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
Old {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural company|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha}} |Foundation = {{ill|lt=Emperor Suiko|Q298057|WD=Q298057}}16th year ({{ill|lt=608th year|Q31037|WD=Q31037}}) |Main shrine = |Alias = {{ill|lt=Shota County|Q11386034|WD=Q11386034}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379}} |Fudasho, etc. = |Regular festival = {{ill|lt=May 15th|Q2562|WD=Q2562}} |Shinto ritual = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Ibaraki|label=Tatenui Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tatenui Shrine'' (Tatenui Shrine) is located at 2988 Gochu [http://www.oyashiro.or.jp/ According to the shrine register of the Ibaraki Prefectural Shrine Office, it is 2988 Kihara, Miura Village. (formerly {{ill|lt=Shota District|Q11386034|WD=Q11386034}}{{ill|lt=Kihara Village|Q11518209|WD=Q11518209}}) {{ill|lt=Shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}}. The old name was {{ill|lt=Shota-gun|Q11386034|WD=Q11386034}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379}}. {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikiuchi-sha|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Hitachi-kuni|Q1055353|WD=Q1055353}} Shinta District, small shrine). Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226}}. There is also Tatenui Shrine with the same name in Shinta Miura Village. === Overview === [[File:Tatenui Shrine (Gochu, Miura Village, Ibaraki Prefecture) 03.jpg|thumb|First Torii]] [[File:Tatenui-jinja shrine Ni no Torii Gate (Miho village, Ibaraki prefecture).jpg|thumb|Ni no Torii Gate]] [[File:Tatenui Shrine (Gochu, Miura Village, Ibaraki Prefecture) 02.jpg|thumb|Chozusha]] The approach road faces the {{ill|lt=National Route 125|Q6156756|WD=Q6156756}} existing road, but the shrine grounds are located in a secluded area and are difficult to find. A long approach path passes through a forest of giant trees, and the shrine building is located at the back. There are no shrine offices or prominent shrines within the precincts, so you can enjoy the simple composition of the grove of trees and shrine buildings.The precincts are designated by Ibaraki Prefecture {{ill|lt=natural environment conservation area|Q11612866|WD=Q11612866}} (29, Ichinomiya). {{ill|lt=Hitachikuni Fudoki|Q11481687|WD=Q11481687}} Fudu myth in the Shinta district sectionThe original text of Hitachikuni Fudoki is ``Takakiri, Koroi, Tenchi Kinkoshi, Plants and trees language no time, the name of the deity who descended from heaven, the title '''Futu Great God''', Junko Ashihara Nakatsukuni, Hehei Mountain River Arakyo no Rui, Great God Transformation Dojinbi, Mind Returns to Heaven, Immediate Zuishin''' Equipment Staff (commonly said, Ikawanogawa Mei), Armored Shield and Shogyokukei, All Take Off''', Detained Kageji, Immediately Riding Hakuun, Returning Soten. It is said to be the place where {{ill|lt=Ketsunushikami|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsu Okami}} took off his shield, which is depicted in , and is therefore the sacred place of ``Tatenui''. There are two legends about the origin of the name Kihara, but both tell that the area has been a rich forest since ancient times. It has a deep relationship with {{ill|lt=Ami Shrine (Takeki, Ami Town)|Q17191825|WD=Q17191825|lt=Takekiami Shrine}} due to its association with the sacred place of Futsu mythology, and in ancient times it was called Ichinomiya Ninomiya as the main shrine for east and west Shinta District, and both {{ill|lt=Eiwa (Japan)|Q1145780|WD=Q1145780|lt=Eiwa}} 1st year (1375) of the Enmyin-ji Shrine and the Shrine and the Sobō Dojojo, which reads "Shonai Daiichi-no-Sobyo" Great Japanese Place Name Dictionary. . Tatenui Shrine was the Shinta District Ichinomiya Shrine, and was one of the most prestigious shrines in the former district area. An ancient ceremony (Kashima Shinto ritual) was also held in which the {{ill|lt=mikoshi|Q979940|WD=Q979940}} of Futsu no Mikoto was carried to {{ill|lt=Ami Shrine|Q16276818|WD=Q16276818}} via Lake Kasumigaura. === Enshrined God === ; Main enshrined deity : Futsu no MikotoMeiji Shrine materials. (Futsusu no Kami, Futsusu Okami) ; Deity enshrined : {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Otomi Takamikoto}} Niza, {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo no Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Ukanomitama|Q308072 8|WD=Q308072 8|lt=Ukatama no Mikoto}} Niza, Kosan Reimyo, {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishima Hime no Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kumano Taisha|Q11568887|WD=Q11568887|lt=Kumano Kaoroki no Mikoto}} * The precinct information board introduces the deeds of {{ill|lt=Futunushi|UNKNOWN|lt=Ami Shrine}} in {{ill|lt=kiki|Q842308|WD=Q842308}}{{ill|lt=mythology|Q12827256|WD=Q12827256}}, and considers the two deities to be the same deity (for Futsu Okami, please refer to {{ill|lt=Ami Shrine (Also see Takeki, Ami Town)|Q17191825|WD=Q17191825|lt=Takekuami Shrine}}). * There is also a separate legend that says the main enshrined deity is {{ill|lt=Hikosa Tomomi|Q135327097|WD=Q135327097}}. * The merger due to shrine reorganization is below Ibaraki Prefecture Shrine Photo Book. . ** {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}}In 1941, merged with Itsukushima Shrine (Ichikishima Hime-no-Mikoto) in Sukazu, Oaza. ** In 1907, Hachiman Shrine ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Honda Betsu no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}) and Atago Shrine (Housan Reimyo Nisha) were merged into the precinct shrine Goryusha. ** In Meiji 45, Inari Shrine (Ugamitama no Mikoto), Orii Shrine ({{ill|lt=Yokamijin|Q644201|WD=Q644201|lt=Koryu no Mikoto}}), and Itsukushima Shrine (Ichikishimahime no Mikoto) were merged into the precinct shrine Goryusha. === History === There is a company biography and a separate biography. In addition to the two theories about its founding, there is another theory that says it was built in 18 AD. . === Company biography === * Founded in {{ill|lt=Emperor Suiko|Q298057|WD=Q298057}}16 (608)Precinct information board. . * Searching for the auspicious origin of creation rituals in Fudu mythology. It is said that after the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=pacification of Ashihara China|1=UNKNOWN}}, Futsu Okami took off his armor at Kihara and ascended to heaven at Takakiri. Because he took off his armor, the place name ``Tatezu'' was born, and later became ``Tatenui.'' The place name ``Tatezuzan'' still remains on the shrine grounds. * The name of the village of Kihara is derived from {{ill|lt=Eisho|Q1193577|WD=Q1193577}} 3rd year (1506), which was built in the precincts of a temple with a circumference of 5 jo, 8 shaku, and a height of 120 shaku.{{jalink |Sugi|Q147388|Cedar}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} withered in May 1868) and {{ill|lt=Kihara Castle (Hitachi Province)|Q11518195|WD=Q11518195|lt=Kihara Castle}}It was born by {{ill|lt=combined place name|Q11412460|WD=Q11412460|lt=combined}} of the ``Hara'' of the lord Kondo Shikibu Daisuke Fujiwara Toshikatsu (Kondo Toshikatsu). Until then, it was called Kamikoshi Village.Precinct information board, Meiji Shrine magazine materials, etc. According to the Miho Village Board of Education's explanation of the cultural property, the origin is a giant cedar tree with a circumference of more than 6 meters located in the north of the precincts. . === Separate story === * The new edition of Hitachi Kokushi states, ``He is said to be the enshrined deity {{ill|lt=Tomomichi Hikosa|Q135327097|WD=Q135327097}}, the guardian of 33 villages in the Middle East of the district, and Ichinomiya of Sokuhon District (shaki, 28 shrines, 28 shrines summary origin) , this god was known as Amaterasu, the ancestor of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ibe|1=UNKNOWN}} of {{ill|lt=Kioku|Q864053|WD=Q864053}}, as well as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tegiho Negami|1=UNKNOWN}}, during the age of the gods. For the great god |Q455602}}, he built a magnificent palace and made various ritual vessels to serve, but since this god only sewed shields, {{jalink|shield sewing|Q11582552|shield sewing} }}It is also said to be a god (Nihongi, Kogo Shui) Even in the human era, there was a tatebe who made shields for ritual utensils.'' (original kana), and the shrine is said to enshrine Hikosa Tomomichi, the ``tatenui deity.'' * The name of the village of Kihara is said to be from an ancient legend that says, ``In the old ancient times, this place was a vast wasteland, with cedar and cypress trees growing here and there, hence the name Kihara.One day, an old man suddenly appeared and told the villagers, ``Your houses are very poor, and when you see this palace, you should build one.'' The old man went under the shade of a cedar tree, entered Tamahinu, followed the worship of the villagers, gathered in front of the shrine, and started his home in this village (28 shrines abbreviated engagement)' (original kana), and it is also said that the Kihara village was formed along with Tatenui Shrine. * In the local history of Inashiki District, there is a statement that says, ``Before the Meiji Restoration, there was a theory that people visited Tatenui Shrine and worshiped it, calling it the ancestral deity of carpenters,'' which suggests that a separate legend had spread in its own way until before the Meiji period. *Generally, the deity enshrined at the shrine called Tatenui Shrine is Hikono Tomomichi. In addition to our company, there were at least four other Tatenui Shrines or Tatebe Shikinai shrines, all of which were shrines of the ``Tatenui God'' (Hikono Chimei).** Tatenui Shrine ({{ill|lt=Tajima Province|Q1053727|WD=Q1053727}}{{ill|lt=Yabu District (Hyogo Prefecture)|Q11666859|WD=Q11666859|lt=Yabu District}}, small shrine). Yabu City, Hyogo Prefecture. Main enshrined deity, Hikono Tomomi ** Tatenui Shrine (Tajima Province {{ill|lt=Keta District (Hyogo Prefecture)|Q21655271|WD=Q21655271|lt=Keta District}}, small shrine). Toyooka City, Hyogo Prefecture. Main enshrined deity, Hikono Tomomi ** Tatenui Shrine ({{ill|lt=Tamba Province|Q954511|WD=Q954511}}{{ill|lt=Hikami District|Q5760344|WD=Q5760344}}, small shrine). Tamba City, Hyogo Prefecture. Main enshrined deity, Hikono Tomomi ** Kawauchi Tatanuhi Shrine (Tamba Province {{ill|lt=Taki District|Q11431006|WD=Q11431006}}, small shrine). Tamba City, Hyogo Prefecture. Main enshrined deity: Amaterasu Omikami
``Tadatanohi'' is a corruption of ``Tatenui,'' and it is said that it was originally a shrine of Tatebe that enshrined Hikonochi no Mikoto. * {{ill|lt=Specially Selected Names of Gods|Q11571092|WD=Q11571092|lt=Specially Selected Names of Gods}} states, ``If the shrine name of Tatenui is used now, it would be as if Hikosa Chi no Mikoto could be enshrined; In the article, ``Futunushi no Mikoto re-sewn the heavenly stone shield (Ame no Ihatate). Therefore, it is called ``Tatenui.'''' edited by Yukichi Takeda. Together with the editorial, it is written that the name ``Tatenui'' is not necessarily derived from ``Tatenui no Mikoto'' (Hikosatomo no Mikoto). === History === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinryo|1=UNKNOWN}} had over 53 towns, but in the first year of {{ill|lt=Kokoku|Q1143592|WD=Q1143592}} (1340), it was invaded by Nobutsune, a subordinate of {{ill|lt=Koshifuyu|Q6455071|WD=Q6455071}} of {{ill|lt=Northern Court (Japan)|Q1201819|WD=Q1201819|lt=Northern Court}}, and became without a living. * It is said that a wooden guardian dog (a cultural property designated by Miho Village) was dedicated to the for the {{ill|lt=Tenbun (era name)|Q1192853|WD=Q1192853|lt=Tenbun}} years (1532-1555). * There are records of reconstruction in {{ill|lt=Bunmei (Japan)|Q1193544|WD=Q1193544|lt=Bunmei}}8 (1476), {{ill|lt=Tensho|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}}17 (1589), and {{ill|lt=Kaei|Q1146125|WD=Q1146125}}7 (1854). The current main hall is said to have been built in Tensho 17 and donated by Toshikatsu Kondo. * In early modern times, Shinta County Ichinomiya was called the shrine of more than 50 villages in the eastern half of Shinta County. * {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}}6 (1873), ranked as a prefectural company. * {{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}}In 1965, the temple of worship was burnt down and ancient documents were lostJoyo Living. . === Nearby shrines === ; Tatenui Shrine : 1830 Shinta, Miho Village, Inashiki District, Ibaraki Prefecture (formerly Shinta Village, Shinta District) : Main enshrined deity, Kyotsu no Mikoto : Deities enshrined: {{ill|lt=Takamimusubi|UNKNOWN|lt=Takōsei Reishin}}, {{ill|lt=Michizane Sugawara|Q382005|WD=Q382005|lt=Michizane Sugawara}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Honda Betsu no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, Takemikazuchi no Mikoto, {{ill|lt=Konohana Sakuyahime|UNKNOWN|lt=Konohana Kaiyahime no Mikoto}}, Uka no Mitama no Mikoto :* The six deities enshrined are jointly enshrined deities (Kousanrei Shrine, Tenma Shrine, Katori Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Ninomiya Kashima Shrine, Sengen Shrine, and Uga Shrine). :* Founding unknown. According to the shrine's biography, it was founded in the {{ill|lt=Emperor Keiko|Q329723|WD=Q329723}} reign (unknown) and rebuilt in the first year (715) of {{ill|lt=Reiki|Q1373115|WD=Q1373115}}Local history of Inashiki district. . Old {{ill|lt=Murasha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. The shrines are Hijo-sha, Shikinai Tatenui-jinja Shrine, and Shinta-gun Sosha (framed). :* Located to the northeast of Shinta Castle Ruins, the area is also a candidate site for Shinta {{ill|lt=gunga|Q11643230|WD=Q11643230}}. === Transportation === Take the JR Bus Kanto from JR Tsuchiura Station, get off at the "Gonaka" bus stop, and walk 10 minutes. === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist|2}} === References === * [https://www.ndl.go.jp/ National Diet Library]: Documents available for viewing from [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/ National Diet Library Digital Collection]. ** Nobuna Nakayama, Hiroshi Kurita, eds. “New edition of Hitachi Kokushi, Vol. 2.” Sekizenkan. Old {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural company|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha}} |Foundation = {{ill|lt=Emperor Suiko|Q298057|WD=Q298057}}16th year ({{ill|lt=608th year|Q31037|WD=Q31037}}) |Main shrine = |Alias = {{ill|lt=Shota County|Q11386034|WD=Q11386034}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379}} |Fudasho, etc. = |Regular festival = {{ill|lt=May 15th|Q2562|WD=Q2562}} |Shinto ritual = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Ibaraki|label=Tatenui Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tatenui Shrine'' (Tatenui Shrine) is located at 2988 Gochu [http://www.oyashiro.or.jp/ According to the shrine register of the Ibaraki Prefectural Shrine Office, it is 2988 Kihara, Miura Village. (formerly {{ill|lt=Shota District|Q11386034|WD=Q11386034}}{{ill|lt=Kihara Village|Q11518209|WD=Q11518209}}) {{ill|lt=Shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}}. The old name was {{ill|lt=Shota-gun|Q11386034|WD=Q11386034}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379}}. {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchi-sha|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Hitachi-kuni|Q1055353|WD=Q1055353}} Shinta District, small shrine). Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226}}. There is also Tatenui Shrine with the same name in Shinta Miura Village. === Overview === [[File:Tatenui Shrine (Gochu, Miura Village, Ibaraki Prefecture) 03.jpg|thumb|First Torii]] [[File:Tatenui-jinja shrine Ni no Torii Gate (Miho village, Ibaraki prefecture).jpg|thumb|Ni no Torii Gate]] [[File:Tatenui Shrine (Gochu, Miura Village, Ibaraki Prefecture) 02.jpg|thumb|Chozusha]] The approach road faces the {{ill|lt=National Route 125|Q6156756|WD=Q6156756}} existing road, but the shrine grounds are located in a secluded area and are difficult to find. A long approach path passes through a forest of giant trees, and the shrine building is located at the back. There are no shrine offices or prominent shrines within the precincts, so you can enjoy the simple composition of the grove of trees and shrine buildings.The precincts are designated by Ibaraki Prefecture {{ill|lt=natural environment conservation area|Q11612866|WD=Q11612866}} (29, Ichinomiya). {{ill|lt=Hitachikuni Fudoki|Q11481687|WD=Q11481687}} Fudu myth in the Shinta district sectionThe original text of Hitachikuni Fudoki is ``Takakiri, Koroi, Tenchi Kinkoshi, Plants and trees language no time, the name of the deity who descended from heaven, the title '''Futu Great God''', Junko Ashihara Nakatsukuni, Hehei Mountain River Arakyo no Rui, Great God Transformation Dojinbi, Mind Returns to Heaven, Immediate Zuishin''' Equipment Staff (commonly said, Ikawanogawa Mei), Armored Shield and Shogyokukei, All Take Off''', Detained Kageji, Immediately Riding Hakuun, Returning Soten. It is said to be the place where {{ill|lt=Ketsunushikami|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsu Okami}} took off his shield, which is depicted in , and is therefore the sacred place of ``Tatenui''. There are two legends about the origin of the name Kihara, but both tell that the area has been a rich forest since ancient times. It has a deep relationship with {{ill|lt=Ami Shrine (Takeki, Ami Town)|Q17191825|WD=Q17191825|lt=Takekiami Shrine}} due to its association with the sacred place of Futsu mythology, and in ancient times it was called Ichinomiya Ninomiya as the main shrine for east and west Shinta District, and both {{ill|lt=Eiwa (Japan)|Q1145780|WD=Q1145780|lt=Eiwa}} 1st year (1375) of the Enmyin-ji Shrine and the Shrine and the Sobō Dojojo, which reads "Shonai Daiichi-no-Sobyo" Great Japanese Place Name Dictionary. . Tatenui Shrine was the Shinta District Ichinomiya Shrine, and was one of the most prestigious shrines in the former district area. An ancient ceremony (Kashima Shinto ritual) was also held in which the {{ill|lt=mikoshi|Q979940|WD=Q979940}} of Futsu no Mikoto was carried to {{ill|lt=Ami Shrine|Q16276818|WD=Q16276818}} via Lake Kasumigaura. === Enshrined God === ; Main enshrined deity : Futsu no MikotoMeiji Shrine materials. (Futsusu no Kami, Futsusu Okami) ; Deity enshrined : {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Otomi Takamikoto}} Niza, {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo no Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Ukanomitama|Q308072 8|WD=Q308072 8|lt=Ukatama no Mikoto}} Niza, Kosan Reimyo, {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishima Hime no Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kumano Taisha|Q11568887|WD=Q11568887|lt=Kumano Kaoroki no Mikoto}} * The precinct information board introduces the deeds of {{ill|lt=Futunushi|UNKNOWN|lt=Ami Shrine}} in {{ill|lt=kiki|Q842308|WD=Q842308}}{{ill|lt=mythology|Q12827256|WD=Q12827256}}, and considers the two deities to be the same deity (for Futsu Okami, please refer to {{ill|lt=Ami Shrine (Also see Takeki, Ami Town)|Q17191825|WD=Q17191825|lt=Takekuami Shrine}}). * There is also a separate legend that says the main enshrined deity is {{ill|lt=Hikosa Tomomi|Q135327097|WD=Q135327097}}. * The merger due to shrine reorganization is below Ibaraki Prefecture Shrine Photo Book. . ** {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}}In 1941, merged with Itsukushima Shrine (Ichikishima Hime-no-Mikoto) in Sukazu, Oaza. ** In 1907, Hachiman Shrine ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Honda Betsu no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}) and Atago Shrine (Housan Reimyo Nisha) were merged into the precinct shrine Goryusha. ** In Meiji 45, Inari Shrine (Ugamitama no Mikoto), Orii Shrine ({{ill|lt=Yokamijin|Q644201|WD=Q644201|lt=Koryu no Mikoto}}), and Itsukushima Shrine (Ichikishimahime no Mikoto) were merged into the precinct shrine Goryusha. === History === There is a company biography and a separate biography. In addition to the two theories about its founding, there is another theory that says it was built in 18 AD. . === Company biography === * Founded in {{ill|lt=Emperor Suiko|Q298057|WD=Q298057}}16 (608)Precinct information board. . * Searching for the auspicious origin of creation rituals in Fudu mythology. It is said that after the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=pacification of Ashihara China|1=UNKNOWN}}, Futsu Okami took off his armor at Kihara and ascended to heaven at Takakiri. Because he took off his armor, the place name ``Tatezu'' was born, and later became ``Tatenui.'' The place name ``Tatezuzan'' still remains on the shrine grounds. * The name of the village of Kihara is derived from {{ill|lt=Eisho|Q1193577|WD=Q1193577}} 3rd year (1506), which was built in the precincts of a temple with a circumference of 5 jo, 8 shaku, and a height of 120 shaku.{{jalink |Sugi|Q147388|Cedar}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} withered in May 1868) and {{ill|lt=Kihara Castle (Hitachi Province)|Q11518195|WD=Q11518195|lt=Kihara Castle}}It was born by {{ill|lt=combined place name|Q11412460|WD=Q11412460|lt=combined}} of the ``Hara'' of the lord Kondo Shikibu Daisuke Fujiwara Toshikatsu (Kondo Toshikatsu). Until then, it was called Kamikoshi Village.Precinct information board, Meiji Shrine magazine materials, etc. According to the Miho Village Board of Education's explanation of the cultural property, the origin is a giant cedar tree with a circumference of more than 6 meters located in the north of the precincts. . === Separate story === * The new edition of Hitachi Kokushi states, ``He is said to be the enshrined deity {{ill|lt=Tomomichi Hikosa|Q135327097|WD=Q135327097}}, the guardian of 33 villages in the Middle East of the district, and Ichinomiya of Sokuhon District (shaki, 28 shrines, 28 shrines summary origin) , this god was known as Amaterasu, the ancestor of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ibe|1=UNKNOWN}} of {{ill|lt=Kioku|Q864053|WD=Q864053}}, as well as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tegiho Negami|1=UNKNOWN}}, during the age of the gods. For the great god |Q455602}}, he built a magnificent palace and made various ritual vessels to serve, but since this god only sewed shields, {{jalink|shield sewing|Q11582552|shield sewing} }}It is also said to be a god (Nihongi, Kogo Shui) Even in the human era, there was a tatebe who made shields for ritual utensils.'' (original kana), and the shrine is said to enshrine Hikosa Tomomichi, the ``tatenui deity.'' * The name of the village of Kihara is said to be from an ancient legend that says, ``In the old ancient times, this place was a vast wasteland, with cedar and cypress trees growing here and there, hence the name Kihara.One day, an old man suddenly appeared and told the villagers, ``Your houses are very poor, and when you see this palace, you should build one.'' The old man went under the shade of a cedar tree, entered Tamahinu, followed the worship of the villagers, gathered in front of the shrine, and started his home in this village (28 shrines abbreviated engagement)' (original kana), and it is also said that the Kihara village was formed along with Tatenui Shrine. * In the local history of Inashiki District, there is a statement that says, ``Before the Meiji Restoration, there was a theory that people visited Tatenui Shrine and worshiped it, calling it the ancestral deity of carpenters,'' which suggests that a separate legend had spread in its own way until before the Meiji period. *Generally, the deity enshrined at the shrine called Tatenui Shrine is Hikono Tomomichi. In addition to our company, there were at least four other Tatenui Shrines or Tatebe Shikinai shrines, all of which were shrines of the ``Tatenui God'' (Hikono Chimei).** Tatenui Shrine ({{ill|lt=Tajima Province|Q1053727|WD=Q1053727}}{{ill|lt=Yabu District (Hyogo Prefecture)|Q11666859|WD=Q11666859|lt=Yabu District}}, small shrine). Yabu City, Hyogo Prefecture. Main enshrined deity, Hikono Tomomi ** Tatenui Shrine (Tajima Province {{ill|lt=Keta District (Hyogo Prefecture)|Q21655271|WD=Q21655271|lt=Keta District}}, small shrine). Toyooka City, Hyogo Prefecture. Main enshrined deity, Hikono Tomomi ** Tatenui Shrine ({{ill|lt=Tamba Province|Q954511|WD=Q954511}}{{ill|lt=Hikami District|Q5760344|WD=Q5760344}}, small shrine). Tamba City, Hyogo Prefecture. Main enshrined deity, Hikono Tomomi ** Kawauchi Tatanuhi Shrine (Tamba Province {{ill|lt=Taki District|Q11431006|WD=Q11431006}}, small shrine). Tamba City, Hyogo Prefecture. Main enshrined deity: Amaterasu Omikami
``Tadatanohi'' is a corruption of ``Tatenui,'' and it is said that it was originally a shrine of Tatebe that enshrined Hikonochi no Mikoto. * {{ill|lt=Specially Selected Names of Gods|Q11571092|WD=Q11571092|lt=Specially Selected Names of Gods}} states, ``If the shrine name of Tatenui is used now, it would be as if Hikosa Chi no Mikoto could be enshrined; In the article, ``Futunushi no Mikoto re-sewn the heavenly stone shield (Ame no Ihatate). Therefore, it is called ``Tatenui.'''' edited by Yukichi Takeda. Together with the editorial, it is written that the name ``Tatenui'' is not necessarily derived from ``Tatenui no Mikoto'' (Hikosatomo no Mikoto). === History === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinryo|1=UNKNOWN}} had over 53 towns, but in the first year of {{ill|lt=Kokoku|Q1143592|WD=Q1143592}} (1340), it was invaded by Nobutsune, a subordinate of {{ill|lt=Koshifuyu|Q6455071|WD=Q6455071}} of {{ill|lt=Northern Court (Japan)|Q1201819|WD=Q1201819|lt=Northern Court}}, and became without a living. * It is said that a wooden guardian dog (a cultural property designated by Miho Village) was dedicated to the for the {{ill|lt=Tenbun (era name)|Q1192853|WD=Q1192853|lt=Tenbun}} years (1532-1555). * There are records of reconstruction in {{ill|lt=Bunmei (Japan)|Q1193544|WD=Q1193544|lt=Bunmei}}8 (1476), {{ill|lt=Tensho|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}}17 (1589), and {{ill|lt=Kaei|Q1146125|WD=Q1146125}}7 (1854). The current main hall is said to have been built in Tensho 17 and donated by Toshikatsu Kondo. * In early modern times, Shinta County Ichinomiya was called the shrine of more than 50 villages in the eastern half of Shinta County. * {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}}6 (1873), ranked as a prefectural company. * {{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}}In 1965, the temple of worship was burnt down and ancient documents were lostJoyo Living. . === Nearby shrines === ; Tatenui Shrine : 1830 Shinta, Miho Village, Inashiki District, Ibaraki Prefecture (formerly Shinta Village, Shinta District) : Main enshrined deity, Kyotsu no Mikoto : Deities enshrined: {{ill|lt=Takamimusubi|UNKNOWN|lt=Takōsei Reishin}}, {{ill|lt=Michizane Sugawara|Q382005|WD=Q382005|lt=Michizane Sugawara}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Honda Betsu no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, Takemikazuchi no Mikoto, {{ill|lt=Konohana Sakuyahime|UNKNOWN|lt=Konohana Kaiyahime no Mikoto}}, Uka no Mitama no Mikoto :* The six deities enshrined are jointly enshrined deities (Kousanrei Shrine, Tenma Shrine, Katori Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Ninomiya Kashima Shrine, Sengen Shrine, and Uga Shrine). :* Founding unknown. According to the shrine's biography, it was founded in the {{ill|lt=Emperor Keiko|Q329723|WD=Q329723}} reign (unknown) and rebuilt in the first year (715) of {{ill|lt=Reiki|Q1373115|WD=Q1373115}}Local history of Inashiki district. . Old {{ill|lt=Murasha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. The shrines are Hijo-sha, Shikinai Tatenui-jinja Shrine, and Shinta-gun Sosha (framed). :* Located to the northeast of Shinta Castle Ruins, the area is also a candidate site for Shinta {{ill|lt=gunga|Q11643230|WD=Q11643230}}. === Transportation === Take the JR Bus Kanto from JR Tsuchiura Station, get off at the "Gonaka" bus stop, and walk 10 minutes. === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist|2}} === References === * [https://www.ndl.go.jp/ National Diet Library]: Documents available for viewing from [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/ National Diet Library Digital Collection]. ** Nobuna Nakayama, Hiroshi Kurita, eds. “New edition of Hitachi Kokushi, Vol. 2.” Sekizenkan. Oratory |Location = {{ill|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture|Q83273|WD=Q83273}}{{ill|lt=Inashiki District|Q1154673|WD=Q1154673}}{{ill|lt=Miho Village|Q1203179|WD=Q1203179}}Gochu 2988 |Position = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = Futoshi no Mikoto |Company rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
Old {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural company|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha}} |Foundation = {{ill|lt=Emperor Suiko|Q298057|WD=Q298057}}16th year ({{ill|lt=608th year|Q31037|WD=Q31037}}) |Main shrine = |Alias = {{ill|lt=Shota County|Q11386034|WD=Q11386034}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379}} |Fudasho, etc. = |Regular festival = {{ill|lt=May 15th|Q2562|WD=Q2562}} |Shinto ritual = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Ibaraki|label=Tatenui Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tatenui Shrine'' (Tatenui Shrine) is located at 2988 Gochu [http://www.oyashiro.or.jp/ According to the shrine register of the Ibaraki Prefectural Shrine Office, it is 2988 Kihara, Miura Village. (formerly {{ill|lt=Shota District|Q11386034|WD=Q11386034}}{{ill|lt=Kihara Village|Q11518209|WD=Q11518209}}) {{ill|lt=Shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}}. The old name was {{ill|lt=Shota-gun|Q11386034|WD=Q11386034}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379}}. {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchi-sha|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Hitachi-kuni|Q1055353|WD=Q1055353}} Shinta District, small shrine). Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226}}. There is also Tatenui Shrine with the same name in Shinta Miura Village. === Overview === [[File:Tatenui Shrine (Gochu, Miura Village, Ibaraki Prefecture) 03.jpg|thumb|First Torii]] [[File:Tatenui-jinja shrine Ni no Torii Gate (Miho village, Ibaraki prefecture).jpg|thumb|Ni no Torii Gate]] [[File:Tatenui Shrine (Gochu, Miura Village, Ibaraki Prefecture) 02.jpg|thumb|Chozusha]] The approach road faces the {{ill|lt=National Route 125|Q6156756|WD=Q6156756}} existing road, but the shrine grounds are located in a secluded area and are difficult to find. A long approach path passes through a forest of giant trees, and the shrine building is located at the back. There are no shrine offices or prominent shrines within the precincts, so you can enjoy the simple composition of the grove of trees and shrine buildings.The precincts are designated by Ibaraki Prefecture {{ill|lt=natural environment conservation area|Q11612866|WD=Q11612866}} (29, Ichinomiya). {{ill|lt=Hitachikuni Fudoki|Q11481687|WD=Q11481687}} Fudu myth in the Shinta district sectionThe original text of Hitachikuni Fudoki is ``Takakiri, Koroi, Tenchi Kinkoshi, Plants and trees language no time, the name of the deity who descended from heaven, the title '''Futu Great God''', Junko Ashihara Nakatsukuni, Hehei Mountain River Arakyo no Rui, Great God Transformation Dojinbi, Mind Returns to Heaven, Immediate Zuishin''' Equipment Staff (commonly said, Ikawanogawa Mei), Armored Shield and Shogyokukei, All Take Off''', Detained Kageji, Immediately Riding Hakuun, Returning Soten. It is said to be the place where {{ill|lt=Ketsunushikami|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsu Okami}} took off his shield, which is depicted in , and is therefore the sacred place of ``Tatenui''. There are two legends about the origin of the name Kihara, but both tell that the area has been a rich forest since ancient times. It has a deep relationship with {{ill|lt=Ami Shrine (Takeki, Ami Town)|Q17191825|WD=Q17191825|lt=Takekiami Shrine}} due to its association with the sacred place of Futsu mythology, and in ancient times it was called Ichinomiya Ninomiya as the main shrine for east and west Shinta District, and both {{ill|lt=Eiwa (Japan)|Q1145780|WD=Q1145780|lt=Eiwa}} 1st year (1375) of the Enmyin-ji Shrine and the Shrine and the Sobō Dojojo, which reads "Shonai Daiichi-no-Sobyo" Great Japanese Place Name Dictionary. . Tatenui Shrine was the Shinta District Ichinomiya Shrine, and was one of the most prestigious shrines in the former district area. An ancient ceremony (Kashima Shinto ritual) was also held in which the {{ill|lt=mikoshi|Q979940|WD=Q979940}} of Futsu no Mikoto was carried to {{ill|lt=Ami Shrine|Q16276818|WD=Q16276818}} via Lake Kasumigaura. === Enshrined God === ; Main enshrined deity : Futsu no MikotoMeiji Shrine materials. (Futsusu no Kami, Futsusu Okami) ; Deity enshrined : {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Otomi Takamikoto}} Niza, {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo no Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Ukanomitama|Q308072 8|WD=Q308072 8|lt=Ukatama no Mikoto}} Niza, Kosan Reimyo, {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishima Hime no Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kumano Taisha|Q11568887|WD=Q11568887|lt=Kumano Kaoroki no Mikoto}} * The precinct information board introduces the deeds of {{ill|lt=Futunushi|UNKNOWN|lt=Ami Shrine}} in {{ill|lt=kiki|Q842308|WD=Q842308}}{{ill|lt=mythology|Q12827256|WD=Q12827256}}, and considers the two deities to be the same deity (for Futsu Okami, please refer to {{ill|lt=Ami Shrine (Also see Takeki, Ami Town)|Q17191825|WD=Q17191825|lt=Takekuami Shrine}}). * There is also a separate legend that says the main enshrined deity is {{ill|lt=Hikosa Tomomi|Q135327097|WD=Q135327097}}. * The merger due to shrine reorganization is below Ibaraki Prefecture Shrine Photo Book. . ** {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}}In 1941, merged with Itsukushima Shrine (Ichikishima Hime-no-Mikoto) in Sukazu, Oaza. ** In 1907, Hachiman Shrine ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Honda Betsu no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}) and Atago Shrine (Housan Reimyo Nisha) were merged into the precinct shrine Goryusha. ** In Meiji 45, Inari Shrine (Ugamitama no Mikoto), Orii Shrine ({{ill|lt=Yokamijin|Q644201|WD=Q644201|lt=Koryu no Mikoto}}), and Itsukushima Shrine (Ichikishimahime no Mikoto) were merged into the precinct shrine Goryusha. === History === There is a company biography and a separate biography. In addition to the two theories about its founding, there is another theory that says it was built in 18 AD. . === Company biography === * Founded in {{ill|lt=Emperor Suiko|Q298057|WD=Q298057}}16 (608)Precinct information board. . * Searching for the auspicious origin of creation rituals in Fudu mythology. It is said that after the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=pacification of Ashihara China|1=UNKNOWN}}, Futsu Okami took off his armor at Kihara and ascended to heaven at Takakiri. Because he took off his armor, the place name ``Tatezu'' was born, and later became ``Tatenui.'' The place name ``Tatezuzan'' still remains on the shrine grounds. * The name of the village of Kihara is derived from {{ill|lt=Eisho|Q1193577|WD=Q1193577}} 3rd year (1506), which was built in the precincts of a temple with a circumference of 5 jo, 8 shaku, and a height of 120 shaku.{{jalink |Sugi|Q147388|Cedar}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} withered in May 1868) and {{ill|lt=Kihara Castle (Hitachi Province)|Q11518195|WD=Q11518195|lt=Kihara Castle}}It was born by {{ill|lt=combined place name|Q11412460|WD=Q11412460|lt=combined}} of the ``Hara'' of the lord Kondo Shikibu Daisuke Fujiwara Toshikatsu (Kondo Toshikatsu). Until then, it was called Kamikoshi Village.Precinct information board, Meiji Shrine magazine materials, etc. According to the Miho Village Board of Education's explanation of the cultural property, the origin is a giant cedar tree with a circumference of more than 6 meters located in the north of the precincts. . === Separate story === * The new edition of Hitachi Kokushi states, ``He is said to be the enshrined deity {{ill|lt=Tomomichi Hikosa|Q135327097|WD=Q135327097}}, the guardian of 33 villages in the Middle East of the district, and Ichinomiya of Sokuhon District (shaki, 28 shrines, 28 shrines summary origin) , this god was known as Amaterasu, the ancestor of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ibe|1=UNKNOWN}} of {{ill|lt=Kioku|Q864053|WD=Q864053}}, as well as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tegiho Negami|1=UNKNOWN}}, during the age of the gods. For the great god |Q455602}}, he built a magnificent palace and made various ritual vessels to serve, but since this god only sewed shields, {{jalink|shield sewing|Q11582552|shield sewing} }}It is also said to be a god (Nihongi, Kogo Shui) Even in the human era, there was a tatebe who made shields for ritual utensils.'' (original kana), and the shrine is said to enshrine Hikosa Tomomichi, the ``tatenui deity.'' * The name of the village of Kihara is said to be from an ancient legend that says, ``In the old ancient times, this place was a vast wasteland, with cedar and cypress trees growing here and there, hence the name Kihara.One day, an old man suddenly appeared and told the villagers, ``Your houses are very poor, and when you see this palace, you should build one.'' The old man went under the shade of a cedar tree, entered Tamahinu, followed the worship of the villagers, gathered in front of the shrine, and started his home in this village (28 shrines abbreviated engagement)' (original kana), and it is also said that the Kihara village was formed along with Tatenui Shrine. * In the local history of Inashiki District, there is a statement that says, ``Before the Meiji Restoration, there was a theory that people visited Tatenui Shrine and worshiped it, calling it the ancestral deity of carpenters,'' which suggests that a separate legend had spread in its own way until before the Meiji period. *Generally, the deity enshrined at the shrine called Tatenui Shrine is Hikono Tomomichi. In addition to our company, there were at least four other Tatenui Shrines or Tatebe Shikinai shrines, all of which were shrines of the ``Tatenui God'' (Hikono Chimei).** Tatenui Shrine ({{ill|lt=Tajima Province|Q1053727|WD=Q1053727}}{{ill|lt=Yabu District (Hyogo Prefecture)|Q11666859|WD=Q11666859|lt=Yabu District}}, small shrine). Yabu City, Hyogo Prefecture. Main enshrined deity, Hikono Tomomi ** Tatenui Shrine (Tajima Province {{ill|lt=Keta District (Hyogo Prefecture)|Q21655271|WD=Q21655271|lt=Keta District}}, small shrine). Toyooka City, Hyogo Prefecture. Main enshrined deity, Hikono Tomomi ** Tatenui Shrine ({{ill|lt=Tamba Province|Q954511|WD=Q954511}}{{ill|lt=Hikami District|Q5760344|WD=Q5760344}}, small shrine). Tamba City, Hyogo Prefecture. Main enshrined deity, Hikono Tomomi ** Kawauchi Tatanuhi Shrine (Tamba Province {{ill|lt=Taki District|Q11431006|WD=Q11431006}}, small shrine). Tamba City, Hyogo Prefecture. Main enshrined deity: Amaterasu Omikami
``Tadatanohi'' is a corruption of ``Tatenui,'' and it is said that it was originally a shrine of Tatebe that enshrined Hikonochi no Mikoto. * {{ill|lt=Specially Selected Names of Gods|Q11571092|WD=Q11571092|lt=Specially Selected Names of Gods}} states, ``If the shrine name of Tatenui is used now, it would be as if Hikosa Chi no Mikoto could be enshrined; In the article, ``Futunushi no Mikoto re-sewn the heavenly stone shield (Ame no Ihatate). Therefore, it is called ``Tatenui.'''' edited by Yukichi Takeda. Together with the editorial, it is written that the name ``Tatenui'' is not necessarily derived from ``Tatenui no Mikoto'' (Hikosatomo no Mikoto). === History === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinryo|1=UNKNOWN}} had over 53 towns, but in the first year of {{ill|lt=Kokoku|Q1143592|WD=Q1143592}} (1340), it was invaded by Nobutsune, a subordinate of {{ill|lt=Koshifuyu|Q6455071|WD=Q6455071}} of {{ill|lt=Northern Court (Japan)|Q1201819|WD=Q1201819|lt=Northern Court}}, and became without a living. * It is said that a wooden guardian dog (a cultural property designated by Miho Village) was dedicated to the for the {{ill|lt=Tenbun (era name)|Q1192853|WD=Q1192853|lt=Tenbun}} years (1532-1555). * There are records of reconstruction in {{ill|lt=Bunmei (Japan)|Q1193544|WD=Q1193544|lt=Bunmei}}8 (1476), {{ill|lt=Tensho|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}}17 (1589), and {{ill|lt=Kaei|Q1146125|WD=Q1146125}}7 (1854). The current main hall is said to have been built in Tensho 17 and donated by Toshikatsu Kondo. * In early modern times, Shinta County Ichinomiya was called the shrine of more than 50 villages in the eastern half of Shinta County. * {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}}6 (1873), ranked as a prefectural company. * {{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}}In 1965, the temple of worship was burnt down and ancient documents were lostJoyo Living. . === Nearby shrines === ; Tatenui Shrine : 1830 Shinta, Miho Village, Inashiki District, Ibaraki Prefecture (formerly Shinta Village, Shinta District) : Main enshrined deity, Kyotsu no Mikoto : Deities enshrined: {{ill|lt=Takamimusubi|UNKNOWN|lt=Takōsei Reishin}}, {{ill|lt=Michizane Sugawara|Q382005|WD=Q382005|lt=Michizane Sugawara}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Honda Betsu no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, Takemikazuchi no Mikoto, {{ill|lt=Konohana Sakuyahime|UNKNOWN|lt=Konohana Kaiyahime no Mikoto}}, Uka no Mitama no Mikoto :* The six deities enshrined are jointly enshrined deities (Kousanrei Shrine, Tenma Shrine, Katori Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Ninomiya Kashima Shrine, Sengen Shrine, and Uga Shrine). :* Founding unknown. According to the shrine's biography, it was founded in the {{ill|lt=Emperor Keiko|Q329723|WD=Q329723}} reign (unknown) and rebuilt in the first year (715) of {{ill|lt=Reiki|Q1373115|WD=Q1373115}}Local history of Inashiki district. . Old {{ill|lt=Murasha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. The shrines are Hijo-sha, Shikinai Tatenui-jinja Shrine, and Shinta-gun Sosha (framed). :* Located to the northeast of Shinta Castle Ruins, the area is also a candidate site for Shinta {{ill|lt=gunga|Q11643230|WD=Q11643230}}. === Transportation === Take the JR Bus Kanto from JR Tsuchiura Station, get off at the "Gonaka" bus stop, and walk 10 minutes. === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist|2}} === References === * [https://www.ndl.go.jp/ National Diet Library]: Documents available for viewing from [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/ National Diet Library Digital Collection]. ** Nobuna Nakayama, Hiroshi Kurita, eds. “New edition of Hitachi Kokushi, Vol. 2.” Sekizenkan.
